# Decisions

Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed.

| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant — any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |
| 2026-04-28 | Phase 1b kept consumer-facing `credentials:*` hooks unchanged; reshape deferred to Phase 3 | Section 7 of the agent-centric design called Phase 1b "mechanical refactor, behavior identical" while Section 3 specified a redesigned shape — internally inconsistent. Picked the conservative reading: ship the seam (`credentials:store-blob:*`) without churning all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 reshapes when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` actually needs `kind`. | Reshape now per Section 3 (rejected: ~600 LOC of churn across credential-proxy + mcp-client + CLI for no Phase 1b benefit) |
| 2026-04-28 | `@ax/credentials-store-db` (Phase 1b) delegates to `storage:get` / `storage:set` rather than owning its own table + migrations | Design Section 3 says "owns the credentials table + Kysely migrations, dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" — but `@ax/database-sqlite` doesn't exist (only postgres has the hook), and the dedicated table earns its weight only with Phase 3 columns (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata`). The seam is still real: vault / KMS backends register `credentials:store-blob:*` against a different store. | Build `@ax/database-sqlite` first (rejected: bigger than Phase 1b); have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (rejected: deferred — wait until Phase 3 schema needs it) |
| 2026-04-29 | Phase A spike confirms D1 (HOME-redirect): runner-owned-sessions design moves forward unchanged | Set HOME=tempdir, ran `query()` one-shot then `query({resume})` — sessionId reattached, magic phrase recalled verbatim. Resume costs ~10× less than turn 1 (validates "replay is wasteful"). Two follow-ups for Phase C: (1) workspace persistence ignore-list keeps only `.claude/projects/`, drops `.claude.json` + backups + policy-limits; (2) `runner:read-transcript` uses sessionId glob, not encoded-cwd reconstruction (SDK uses `realpath(cwd)`, breaks on macOS `/var` → `/private/var`). | Bind-mount `~/.claude/projects/<cwd>` to a workspace path (rejected: more sandbox-specific plumbing, doesn't generalize to k8s — see design D1) |
| 2026-04-29 | No v1→v2 schema migrations in ax-next, ever | ax-next is greenfield with no production data to preserve. The "forward-only via future v2 side-table, never an in-place ALTER" rule in `conversations_v1` was inherited from a different mental model. ALTER TABLE in place is correct now AND for every future schema change. The `_v1` suffix on existing tables (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`, etc.) is now just a stable identifier, not a version pointer. Confirmed by Vinay 2026-04-29 during Phase B planning. | Maintain v1 → v2 split for breaking changes (rejected: greenfield, no rows to migrate; speculative versioning earns nothing) |
| 2026-05-21 | Finding 3 facade lives in `@ax/core` (`registerWorkspaceApplyFacade`); backends rename their raw hook to `workspace:apply-internal`; callers of `workspace:apply` are untouched | Facade logic (fire pre-apply → call internal → fire applied) is backend-agnostic, so core is the principled single home (a future GCS backend reuses it). Renaming the *registration* (not the call) means every existing `call('workspace:apply')` site routes through the facade with zero churn — the cleanest way to make the policy chokepoint unbypassable. `filterToPolicy` relocates to core (only depends on the `FileChange` core type) so the facade can policy-filter pre-apply identically to commit-notify. Full design: `docs/plans/2026-05-21-finding3-workspace-apply-facade.md`. | Per-backend duplicated facade (rejected: violates one-source-of-truth); facade in `@ax/workspace-git-core` (rejected: not backend-agnostic — GCS won't import it); also route `apply-bundle`/commit-notify through the facade (rejected: commit-notify already fires both hooks, not a bypasser; out of finding-3 scope) |
| 2026-05-21 | New `yolo-ship` skill is an **orchestrator that sequences existing skills**, not a re-implementation | The brainstorm/design/implement/ship phases map 1:1 onto superpowers:brainstorming / writing-plans / subagent-driven-development / finishing-a-development-branch + commit-push-pr. The skill adds only the autonomy contract + the CodeRabbit/CI ship loop, and cross-references the rest by name (no @ links, per writing-skills). | Write a self-contained monolith re-explaining each phase (rejected: duplicates skills that already exist + evolve independently; bloats context) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` keeps context low via **subagent-driven implementation + Explore for reads**; orchestrator holds only plan + memory + summaries | User's hard cap is < 300–400K tokens across all three phases. Per-task implementation subagents give ~50–100× savings; codebase exploration goes through Explore so file dumps never hit the orchestrator. | One long-running orchestrator agent doing everything inline (rejected: just relocates the context blowup); fully delegate brainstorm+design to a subagent too (rejected: orchestrator's judgment + memory are most valuable in those phases) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` runs brainstorming in **self-answering mode** and logs decisions instead of asking the user | User instruction: "don't ask questions unless absolutely necessary — trust your recommendations as long as you document them in memory." Brainstorming is normally interactive; here the orchestrator generates the questions, answers from code/memory/conventions, and records each in decisions.md. AskUserQuestion reserved for high-stakes + ambiguous + non-inferable. | Keep brainstorming interactive (rejected: contradicts the autonomy instruction) |
| 2026-05-21 | `yolo-ship` is a **project skill** (`.claude/skills/`, committed) and **`done = green review-clean PR, NOT merged`** | It wires into this repo's CodeRabbit + TODO.md + `.claude/memory/` conventions, so it belongs with the other day-1 project skills, not `~/.claude/skills`. Stop condition mirrors the user's spec exactly ("if everything looks good… you are done") — no auto-merge. | Personal skill in `~/.claude/skills` (rejected: CodeRabbit/TODO/memory wiring is repo-specific); auto-merge on green (rejected: user said stop at done) |
| 2026-05-21 | UI-bugs fix: implement the 5 small edits **inline** rather than via per-task subagents | yolo-ship mandates subagent-driven impl to protect orchestrator context, but the rationale is heavy exploration tasks. I already did the deep reads (root causes confirmed; ~5 surgical files). Spawning subagents to re-derive known context would cost MORE total tokens and risk divergence. Context budget stays well under the 300–400K cap. | Strict one-subagent-per-task (rejected here: overhead/divergence risk for tiny, fully-specified diffs) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug: replace the one-shot `assistantTurnCount !== 1` gate with a **bounded retry** (`<= MAX_TITLE_ATTEMPT_TURNS`, =3) while untitled | Root cause of "inconsistent" titles is the single attempt: if it's skipped/fails for ANY transient reason (jsonl-read race in `conversations:get`, llm:call error, validation rejection) the chat is permanently untitled. `title !== null` guard + `ifNull:true` on set-title already make re-attempts idempotent, so retrying on the next few assistant turns is safe; the cap bounds title-LLM spend if a model keeps emitting unusable titles. | Keep one-shot + only fix the UI (rejected: leaves "never generated" class unfixed); unbounded retry (rejected: unbounded LLM spend on degenerate output) |
| 2026-05-21 | Title bug (UI half): widen `generateTitle` poll window (more attempts + backoff, ~12-15s) instead of a client-side first-message fallback title | The 3×1s poll often gives up before the backend round-trip (jsonl read + haiku) lands, permanently caching "New Chat" until a full reload. Widening catches the common case. A client fallback title (first-message snippet) was rejected: it would later flip to the LLM title on reload — jarring. Residual (title landing after the widened window still needs a list() refresh) tracked as TODO. | Client-side fallback title (rejected: diverges from backend, flips on reload); push/websocket title update (rejected: out of scope for MVP) |
| 2026-05-21 | Attachment chip bug: **reconstruct the `attachment` block from the stored text-mention on the host read path** (`conversations:get`), gated to the conversation's own `.ax/uploads/<conversationId>/` prefix | Under runner-owned-sessions the jsonl stores the runner's *translation* (text-mention `User attached '<name>' at <path> (<mime>)`), not the original `attachment` block — so the original is genuinely gone. The mention embeds path+name+mime (everything the chip needs; `sizeBytes` is dropped by the render path anyway). Reconstruction needs no schema/table and **retroactively fixes already-broken chats** (no migration). Format owned by a shared `formatAttachmentMention`/`parseAttachmentMention` pair in `@ax/ipc-protocol` so producer (runner) and consumer (conversations) can't drift. Path-prefix gate + `AttachmentBlockSchema.safeParse` + role==='user'-only keep untrusted jsonl from injecting an out-of-scope downloadable ref. | Host-side per-turn attachment-block table overlaid on read (rejected: new table+hook+turnId-matching, heavier, and needs a migration to fix old chats; `sizeBytes` is the only extra fidelity and it's unused by the render path); client-side parse in blocksToParts (rejected: leaves the wire shape lossy for all other consumers of conversations:get) |
| 2026-05-21 | `@ax/web-tools` impl: branch the worktree off `feat/web-tools` HEAD (not main); PR base = `main`, carrying the design-spec + impl-plan doc commits into the PR | The two doc commits (497fb157, 450153bb) are part of this feature and belong in the same PR. Branching off main would orphan them. | Open PR against feat/web-tools (rejected: CodeRabbit only reviews PRs based on main); separate docs PR (rejected: needless split) |
| 2026-05-21 | Verified all 14 load-bearing plan claims via one Explore agent before implementing — grounding TRUE (core exports, `PluginError` code union incl. `invalid-payload`/`init-failed`, host-exec contract = full ToolCall + `{output}` wrap, `^0.91.1`, preset insertion points) | Plan itself flagged the `memory-search` `input.query` bug; don't trust signatures blindly. Item-14 Explore self-contradiction ("HookBus unwraps the payload") is WRONG — item 6 is ground truth: web-tools reads `call.input`, mirroring mcp-client, NOT memory-search. | Trust the plan as-written (rejected: cheap to verify; a wrong signature would fail a subagent mid-chain) |
| 2026-05-21 | Implement via ~6 **sequential** subagents grouped by coherent file-unit (scaffold / url-guard+anthropic-client / two tool executors / plugin+exports / canary / preset-wiring), not strict 1-per-12-tasks | Plan gives complete code per step; grouping keeps TDD checkpoints + the plan's per-step commits intact while cutting orchestration round-trips. Subagents share the worktree fs, so they MUST run sequentially (each builds on prior commits), never parallel. | Strict 1-subagent-per-task (rejected: 12 round-trips for a paint-by-numbers plan); inline (rejected: blows orchestrator context budget) |
| 2026-05-21 | `web_extract` output is `{ url, title?, text }` — drop the `truncated` field the design spec listed | Impl plan (Task 4 + Task 12 Step 3) resolves the spec/plan discrepancy by dropping `truncated` and syncing the spec: `max_content_tokens` already caps content server-side and a bare boolean adds no actionable signal for the agent. | Keep `truncated` (rejected: no consumer; would require tracking server-side clipping we don't observe) |
| 2026-05-21 | Scope the web-tools PR to web-tools only: rebase `feat/web-tools-impl --onto origin/main`, dropping the 2 unrelated commits `b3c87929` (auth-better baseURL) + `494b9e7c` (channel-web pill spacing) that rode on `feat/web-tools` | Reviewer-disciplined / scoped-PR convention (Vinay's documented style): a "web-tools" PR shouldn't carry unrelated fixes — they'd muddy CodeRabbit's review + the PR narrative. Branch lacked only PR #120 with zero file overlap, so the rebase is conflict-free. The 2 dropped commits are PRESERVED on the untouched original `feat/web-tools` branch; flagged in TODO + final report so the user can ship them separately. | Keep them in the PR (rejected: scope creep, unrelated review surface); ask the user (rejected: inferable from convention; commits preserved either way) |
| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE: worktree branched **fresh from origin/main** (drops the 2 unpushed local commits f1e2deb7 channel-web spacing + c168fb8c auth baseURL — same pair as entry 27, recommitted) for a clean PR diff | Same reviewer-disciplined scoped-PR convention as entry 27; those 2 fixes don't intersect the title-SSE files. They stay on local `main` for the user to push separately. | Branch from local HEAD (rejected: PR diff would carry the 2 unrelated fixes) |
| 2026-05-21 | Live-title-SSE design (user-approved + autonomous): **per-user** route `GET /api/chat/title-events` (one connection covers all sidebar rows); fire `conversations:title-updated` from the **set-title write point** (one source of truth, covers future rename UI); client **updates the row in place** via new `session-store.applyTitle` + **bumpVersion()-on-connect resync** (no server replay buffer); mount the consumer in **AppContent** not Sidebar (Sidebar unmounts when admin panel opens). Additive — existing client poll untouched. Plan docs allowlisted in `.gitignore` (project default ignores `docs/plans/*`). | Per-user matches the sidebar's need; set-title is the only title writer; direct-update avoids a refetch per frame while onOpen-resync covers disconnect gaps; AppContent is the stable post-auth shell. In-process bus (single-replica) matches the chat-SSE precedent — cross-replica deferred (design I6). | Per-conversation SSE (rejected: N connections); fire from conversation-titles (rejected: not the write point); refetch-per-frame (rejected: wasteful); replace the poll (rejected: user chose additive) |
| 2026-05-22 | Credentialed-CLI/git-auth (yolo-ship of `docs/plans/2026-05-22-credentialed-cli-tools-and-git-auth-design.md`): **`GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0` is already stamped** in both `sandbox-k8s/pod-spec.ts` (`gitParanoidEnv`) and `sandbox-subprocess/open-session.ts` — design §4.3/§4.6 asked to add it; cut to a **regression assertion** instead of re-stamping | Verified by Explore against current code: both sandbox providers already set it (value `'0'`) and `proxy-startup.ts` forwards `GIT_*` by prefix (`ENV_ALLOWLIST_PREFIXES=['GIT_','LC_']`). Re-adding would be a no-op edit. YAGNI cut per [[feedback_yagni_check_in_plans]]; a guard test catches accidental removal. | Re-stamp it (rejected: already present, dead edit); skip even the assertion (rejected: it's load-bearing for B's fail-fast, cheap to lock in) |
| 2026-05-22 | Sub-project B Basic-auth transform uses a **per-request re-arming HTTP/1.1 framing state machine** in the MITM client→upstream path, not single-head-per-connection as design §4.2 literally describes | git smart-HTTP reuses ONE CONNECT tunnel for `GET /info/refs?service=git-upload-pack` (no body) **then** `POST /git-upload-pack` (body), and with creds in the URL libcurl sends `Authorization: Basic` **preemptively on both**. Single-head buffering would transform the GET but leave the POST's base64 placeholder corrupted → fetch fails after refs succeed. State machine: buffer head→transform Basic→`Content-Length`-counted body then re-arm to head; `Transfer-Encoding: chunked` or oversized head → flush + verbatim passthrough, no re-arm (the chunked POST is the clone's last request, so not re-arming after it is safe). Header-only rewrite; bodies keep existing verbatim per-chunk sub (honors I1). | Single head per connection (rejected: corrupts the POST auth on keep-alive); full chunked de-framing to re-arm after chunked bodies (rejected: YAGNI — git's chunked POST is terminal; adds a chunk-size parser for no MVP gain); line-scan for `Authorization:` anywhere in the stream (rejected: false-matches inside POST bodies could corrupt them) |

Design §4.5 mandates parity. Extract a small `scanForCanary(text, tokens)` helper so head-transform and the existing per-chunk path share it. | Leave canary scan only on raw bytes (rejected: the new decode step blinds it — a real exfil gap) |

Design §5.8 path is correct; project-memory note ("parser at packages/skills") is the shim. `packages` grammar, caps, and new error codes go in skills-parser; `EMPTY_CAPABILITIES` + `SkillCapabilities` type gain `packages`; resolve carries it automatically (re-parse on every `skills:resolve`). | Edit the `@ax/skills` shim (rejected: not the implementation) |
| 2026-05-22 | D rejects unsupported ecosystems (`packages.go`) and malformed names with **new `ManifestCode` entries** `invalid-package` (bad name shape / caps) + `unsupported-package-ecosystem` (go and any non-npm/pypi key) | Mirrors the existing per-feature code pattern (`invalid-mcp-command`, `invalid-mcp-transport`); a clear distinct code lets the admin UI message precisely ("Go isn't supported yet" vs "package name invalid"). Grammar shaped so adding `go` later is just removing it from the unsupported set + a registry-host union. | One generic `invalid-package` for both (rejected: can't distinguish "not yet supported" from "malformed"); silently drop unknown ecosystems (rejected: I4/invariant — untrusted manifest content must fail loudly, not be ignored) |

Only uncommitted changes are untracked/gitignored (decisions.md, `.claude/prompts/`, etc.) which targeted `git add` won't sweep in — isolation is already effective. | Separate git worktree per yolo-ship default (rejected: gitignored plan not carried + costly reinstall for no real isolation gain) |

content.content:
1	# Mistakes
2	
3	One entry per mistake. Graduates to `## Resolved` after two subsequent sessions avoid it. Never deleted.
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` — **Shebang alone isn't enough for `pnpm exec` / direct invocation.** `@ax/cli` `dist/main.js` had `#!/usr/bin/env node` but was not executable; `pnpm exec ax-next` failed. Fix: `chmod +x dist/main.js` in the postbuild script (commit 3488654). Next time: when scaffolding a CLI package, add the chmod to postbuild at scaffold time.
6	- `2026-04-23` — **`require.main === module` doesn't work in ESM.** `@ax/cli` main-module guard misfired under ESM. Fix: use `pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href === import.meta.url` (commit 87246cc). Next time: any ESM entry-point pattern uses `pathToFileURL`, not CJS idioms.
7	- `2026-04-23` — **kysely `^0.27.4` pulled GHSA-wmrf / GHSA-8cpq advisories.** Bumped to `^0.28.14` post-merge (commit 957288b). Next time: before pinning a new dep, run `pnpm audit` or check `npm view <pkg> --json` for advisories on the target version range, not just latest.
8	- `2026-05-03` — **PR #37 wired plugins into the preset but didn't update the chart's env-var stamping; tests didn't catch it because they sidestep the production listener layout.** `@ax/http-server` + `@ax/auth-oidc` + `@ax/teams` + `@ax/agents` + `@ax/channel-web` shipped into the preset, but `deployment.yaml` never set `AX_HTTP_HOST` / `AX_HTTP_PORT` / `AX_HTTP_COOKIE_KEY` / `AX_DEV_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN`. Three plugins also collided on port 8080 (`EADDRINUSE`). Fix in PR #40: `serve` registers routes via `http:register-route` (no third listener); chart-shape test scans `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `env.*` reads + asserts every required var is in the deployment + every deployment env is read somewhere. Caught and removed an orphan `K8S_IMAGE_PULL_POLICY` env on the way. Next time: when wiring a new plugin into the preset, the chart-shape test is the contract — if it would catch the gap, ship it in the same PR.
9	- `2026-05-16` — **Walked Phase C MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE expecting the runner→host workspace sync chain to be live; it isn't in current main.** Spent two chat rounds asking the agent to write `.ax/routines/notify.md`, watched the runner pod spawn + exit cleanly, saw `routines_v1_definitions` stay at zero rows. Root cause: Default Agent has `workspace_ref=NULL` (`packages/onboarding/src/completion-tx.ts:96-110` doesn't pass one), and the chat orchestrator explicitly defers `workspaceRef` consumption (`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts:844-852`: "leave workspaceRef unconsumed"). No plugin in the active preset wires session-close → bundle export → workspace apply. Tracked as issue #79. Next time: before walking any MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE scenario that depends on `workspace:applied` firing from an agent-written file, grep `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `commit-notify` / `bundle` / `workspace:apply` wiring first — if nothing connects session-close to apply, the scenario will fail regardless of what your PR shipped. Also: the doc's `/workspace` references are out of date (runner has `/permanent` + `/ephemeral` + `/tmp`, no `/workspace`).
10

Better: treat "PR opened" and "tests pass on a significant change" as hard write-phase triggers, not soft hints. At minimum, ask the meta-question ("what did I learn about how I work?") before declaring a task done.
6	- `2026-04-29` — **I offered the user an option that depended on local fs state without checking that state first.** Asked "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY vs ~/.claude/.credentials.json", user picked the credentials path — and then I discovered macOS keeps creds in Keychain, not on disk. # Meta — self-observations about how I work on this project

Behaviors (not project facts). Name a behavior, name the better alternative. Tag `active` if load-bearing for next session.

- `2026-04-23` `active` — **I've been skipping the claude-memory write phase on task completion.** Week 3 shipped as PR #2 without any memory write; this bootstrap is retroactive. Better: treat "PR opened" and "tests pass on a significant change" as hard write-phase triggers, not soft hints. At minimum, ask the meta-question ("what did I learn about how I work?") before declaring a task done.
- `2026-04-29` — **I offered the user an option that depended on local fs state without checking that state first.** Asked "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY vs ~/.claude/.credentials.json", user picked the credentials path — and then I discovered macOS keeps creds in Keychain, not on disk. Better: when listing options that hinge on a local file/binary/env-var existing, do the existence check inline before the AskUserQuestion. One extra Bash call upfront beats a wasted question round-trip.

12	(isolated worktree off origin/main), **PR #137**, base main. **CI green (all 7

16	correcting the acceptance grep (CodeRabbit conflated the bus event name/`reason`
17	with the `pod_exited`/`pod_killed` watcher lines; CodeRabbit confirmed the
18	correction). **NOT merged** (yolo-ship stops at green+clean). MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE
19	walk still deferred.
20	
21	**Bug:** kill the serving runner pod mid-turn → host logs `pod_exited code:143`
22	but the client SSE got no error/`done` frame → "Thinking…" spinner hung forever
23	(the 25s keepalive holds the stream open). Root cause: the SSE handler only emits
24	a terminal frame on `chat:turn-end`; a *terminated* outcome fires `chat:end`
25	(audit) but never `chat:turn-end`.
26	
27	**Fix (one mechanism, end-to-end):** new broadcast **`chat:turn-error {reqId,
28	reason}`** fired by the orchestrator → per-connection SSE subscriber (match by
29	**reqId**, unlike turn-end which matches conversationId — the orchestrator
30	carries the original agent:invoke reqId) → new `SseFrame {reqId, error}` + close.
31	Client transport emits an AI-SDK `{type:'error', errorText}` chunk (terminates
32	turn, `running`→false); runtime `useChat({onError})` → `applyTurnError` flips
33	`agentStatusActions` to error + **retry**=`regenerate()`. `agentStatusActions.error`
34	existed but was wired to NOTHING in prod before this.
35	
36	**Two fire points** (robust across keepalive-default k8s): (a) the
37	`if(!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` chokepoints in BOTH fresh-spawn + routed
38	paths (sandbox-exit + wedged-runner timeout); (b) a new **`session:terminate`
39	subscriber** firing turn-error for in-flight `!settled` reqIds — covers the
40	routed/warm path PROMPTLY (it doesn't watch handle.exited; else it'd hang until
41	the 10-min chatTimeoutMs). The subscriber does NOT resolve the deferred — the
42	existing timeout path still fires the single chat:end (audit invariant); a later
43	dup turn-error is a no-op (SSE already closed).
44	
45	**Scope:** Fault A + wedged-timeout only. Faults **B/D** (host bounce / net drop
46	→ silent finalize) are a SEPARATE finding (transport `flush()` synthesizes a
47	done-less finish) — left as their own TODO, not folded in.
48	
49	**Files:** chat-orchestrator (orchestrator.ts fireTurnError + onSessionTerminate;
50	plugin.ts subscribes session:terminate), channel-web server (types.ts SseFrame
51	error variant, sse.ts per-conn subscriber, plugin.ts subscribes + evictor),
52	channel-web client (transport.ts ERROR_LABELS+parse, runtime.tsx onError, new
53	turn-error.ts helper). +2 new files. RED→GREEN tests in all 4 areas.
54	
55	**Deferred:** MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE live walk on kind (added as a scenario in
56	deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md) — host-side TS so it rides the fast hostPath dist
57	loop, no image rebuild; not yet walked.

59	**MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE WALK DONE 2026-05-24 (PR #137 merged, image rebuilt from main).**
60	Built+loaded ax-next/agent:dev from the merged source, rollout-restart host,
61	verified the fix markers in the running pod (`chat_turn_error` in orchestrator.js,
62	`chat:turn-error` in sse.js/plugin.js, "stopped unexpectedly. Retry" in the SPA
63	bundle web/assets). Auth = minted `ax_auth_session` from a live
64	auth_better_v1_sessions token signed via http-server parseCookieKey+signCookieValue
65	(per [[reference_headless_authed_chat_kind]]), planted via Playwright
66	`document.cookie=` (HttpOnly not enforced on JS writes), DB is `ax_next`.
67	
68	- **PRIMARY (Fault A core) = PASS.** New chat → long prompt → killed serving pod
69	  (`kubectl -n ax-next-runners delete pod <serving> --now`) mid-turn → within ~1s
70	  the AgentStatus row flipped to a red "The agent stopped unexpectedly. Retry to
71	  continue." + retry button (NOT an infinite spinner). Host log:
72	  `pod_exited` code:143 → `chat_turn_error` reason `sandbox-exit-before-chat-end`
73	  (1ms later) → `pod_killed`, all on the same reqId. Screenshot
74	  faultA-walk-error-retry.png. Normal chat (fresh "pong") also completes on the
75	  rebuilt image; retry MECHANISM fires (regenerate → POST 202 → new SSE → fresh pod).
76	
77	- **Follow-up F1 — STICKY ERROR ROW (client regression from this PR).** Once
78	  `agentStatusActions` enters error mode it never resets on a later turn:
79	  `RunningEffect` (channel-web AgentStatus.tsx) only does hidden→working on mount
80	  and working→hidden on unmount — never error→working. So after ANY turn-error the
81	  status row stays stuck (stale text + retry button, error styling) through the
82	  retry turn AND all subsequent turns/new-sessions until a full page reload (reload
83	  clears it — it's in-memory module state). `agentStatusActions.error` was dead in
84	  prod before #137, so this only surfaces now. Fix: RunningEffect mount should reset
85	  a stale 'error'→'working' ('Thinking…') when a new turn starts; +agent-status test.
86	
87	- **Follow-up F2 — runner crash on retry-replay (code 1) NOT surfaced.** The retry's
88	  runner pod crashed on its own (`pod_exited` code:1, ~2s after pod_ready) — likely
89	  the runner choking replaying a conversation whose last turn was interrupted
90	  mid-stream (separate runner-side bug, adjacent to runner-owned-sessions/transcript
91	  PRs #125/#127/#132). Crucially it fired NO `chat_turn_error` → client stuck on
92	  "Starting sandbox…". Residual turn-error coverage gap: not every abnormal end
93	  surfaces. Most likely cause = the runner POSTs event.chat-end with a terminated
94	  outcome before crashing → onChatEnd resolves the deferred (resolvedByChatEndSubscriber
95	  stays true) → the `if(!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber)` fireTurnError is skipped, and
96	  no turn-end fires → SSE hangs. Fixing needs onChatEnd (or chat:end subscriber) to
97	  surface a turn-error on a terminated runner-reported outcome — but matched by
98	  conversationId (the IPC-fired chat:end restamps reqId; see the reqId-vs-
99	  conversationId matching note in decisions.md). Early-spawn terminated paths
100	  (queue-work-failed / sandbox-open-failed) are also unwired (scoped out of #137).
101	
102	**Handoff prompt for F1+F2:** `.claude/prompts/fault-a-followups-f1-f2.md` (main
103	checkout) — self-contained; F1 (RunningEffect error→working reset) + F2b (onChatEnd
104	fires turn-error matched by conversationId + wire early-spawn returns) are in scope;
105	F2a (runner crash on interrupted-transcript replay) needs runner-side investigation +
106	image-rebuild verify.
107	
108	**Gotcha hit this run:** my first test Edit used a `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/...`
109	absolute path = the MAIN checkout, NOT the worktree (`.../.claude/worktrees/
110	fault-a-sse-turn-error/...`). Reverted main with `git checkout --`, re-applied to
111	the worktree. decisions.md DOES belong in the main checkout (canonical). Lesson:
112	in a worktree, code edits MUST use the worktree path prefix.

1	---
2	name: project_chat_qa_sweep_2026_05_23
3	description: Second chat-qa-sweep run (main@8b4a93c8) — found systemic swallowed-error pattern + two chip read-path bugs
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 8b4de4ec-564b-45f8-b980-ceb474381b8f
8	---
9	
10	2026-05-23 chat-qa-sweep on kind ax-next-dev, main@8b4a93c8, agent "Default Agent". Result: 8 PASS · 3 GLITCH · 4 FAIL · 4 SKIP. Happy path solid (new chat, response, upload, title, parallel-isolation, cancel, double-submit, hostile-input/XSS all PASS).
11	
12	**Headline finding — UI swallows backend failures across all testable fault classes:**
13	- **Fault A (sandbox kill mid-turn) = FAIL, highest priority.** Killed serving runner; host logs `pod_exited code:143` but the SSE never gets an error/`done` frame → "Thinking…" spinner hangs ~155s, no error surface. Recoverable only via manual Stop (then next msg works); NOT self-healing. Fix: host should emit a turn-error frame on mid-turn `pod_exited`.
14	- **Faults B (host `rollout restart`) + D (drop port-forward) = GLITCH.** No white-screen/hang and both recover cleanly, but the interrupted turn **silently finalizes** (Copy/Retry enabled, status idle) with no visible connection-lost/retry error. Client synthesizes a `done`-less-close finish but surfaces nothing. Likely one shared fix.
15	
16	**Two chip read-path bugs:**
17	- **#7 FAIL** — `artifact_publish` succeeds server-side but chip renders **"Unknown artifact"** and clicking fires no download/no error. screenshot qa-07-artifact-unknown.png.
18	- **#8(c)/#6 FAIL** — on reload (`conversations:get`), uploaded attachment comes back as raw model-view text (`User attached 'qa-marker.txt' (text/plain, 139 bytes): <content>`) instead of a reconstructed downloadable chip — jsonl model-view leaking into user-view (the area [[project_ui_bugs_pr120_shipped]] touched). Messages/tool-call/context-continuity reconstruct fine. screenshot qa-08-reload-attachment-as-text.png.
19	
20	**SKIPs (not bugs):** #3 npx→403 (agent lacks `packages` capability, egress lock working as designed; UI surfaced it cleanly); #4 no skill attached (github skill is `default_attached=f`); #14 `/error` dev triggers are DEV-build-only (`Composer.tsx:112 if(!import.meta.env.DEV) return`), kind serves prod bundle; **C (provider error) = SKIP-safety** — chat resolves `provider:anthropic` via shared global DB credential (`credential:v2:global:_:provider:anthropic` in storage_postgres_v1_kv) that shadows env fallback (`credentials/src/plugin.ts:498-544`), so firing requires corrupting the shared cred + env + host bounces → unsafe on the live cluster. Credential left untouched.
21	
22	Cluster left clean (restore checklist all green; Anthropic credential intact). Each FAIL/GLITCH still needs a fix PR + regression test per CLAUDE.md bug-fix policy. Compare to first sweep [[project_chat_qa_sweep_skill_built]] (found transcript-loss → PR #132).

2026-05-23 chat-qa-sweep on kind ax-next-dev, main@8b4a93c8, agent "Default Agent". Result: 8 PASS · 3 GLITCH · 4 FAIL · 4 SKIP. Happy path solid (new chat, response, upload, title, parallel-isolation, cancel, double-submit, hostile-input/XSS all PASS).

**Headline finding — UI swallows backend failures across all testable fault classes:**
- **Fault A (sandbox kill mid-turn) = FAIL, highest priority.** Killed serving runner; host logs `pod_exited code:143` but the SSE never gets an error/`done` frame → "Thinking…" spinner hangs ~155s, no error surface. Recoverable only via manual Stop (then next msg works); NOT self-healing. Fix: host should emit a turn-error frame on mid-turn `pod_exited`.
- **Faults B (host `rollout restart`) + D (drop port-forward) = GLITCH.** No white-screen/hang and both recover cleanly, but the interrupted turn **silently finalizes** (Copy/Retry enabled, status idle) with no visible connection-lost/retry error. Client synthesizes a `done`-less-close finish but surfaces nothing. Likely one shared fix.

**Two chip read-path bugs:**
- **#7 FAIL** — `artifact_publish` succeeds server-side but chip renders **"Unknown artifact"** and clicking fires no download/no error. screenshot qa-07-artifact-unknown.png.
- **#8(c)/#6 FAIL** — on reload (`conversations:get`), uploaded attachment comes back as raw model-view text (`User attached 'qa-marker.txt' (text/plain, 139 bytes): <content>`) instead of a reconstructed downloadable chip — jsonl model-view leaking into user-view (the area [[project_ui_bugs_pr120_shipped]] touched). Messages/tool-call/context-continuity reconstruct fine. screenshot qa-08-reload-attachment-as-text.png.

**SKIPs (not bugs):** #3 npx→403 (agent lacks `packages` capability, egress lock working as designed; UI surfaced it cleanly); #4 no skill attached (github skill is `default_attached=f`); #14 `/error` dev triggers are DEV-build-only (`Composer.tsx:112 if(!import.meta.env.DEV) return`), kind serves prod bundle; **C (provider error) = SKIP-safety** — chat resolves `provider:anthropic` via shared global DB credential (`credential:v2:global:_:provider:anthropic` in storage_postgres_v1_kv) that shadows env fallback (`credentials/src/plugin.ts:498-544`), so firing requires corrupting the shared cred + env + host bounces → unsafe on the live cluster. Credential left untouched.

Cluster left clean (restore checklist all green; Anthropic credential intact). Each FAIL/GLITCH still needs a fix PR + regression test per CLAUDE.md bug-fix policy. Compare to first sweep [[project_chat_qa_sweep_skill_built]] (found transcript-loss → PR #132).

content.content:
1	# Project context
2	
3	Confirmed facts about ax-next. Not opinions.
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` — `@ax/cli` is the only package allowed to import sibling plugins; enforced by `eslint.config.mjs` exception. All other inter-plugin communication goes through the hook bus.
6	- `2026-04-23` — Storage hook payload contract is `{ key: string, value: Uint8Array }` — opaque bytes, no backend vocab. Any `table`/`bucket`/`rowid` field would leak.
7	- `2026-04-23` — pnpm 10 requires `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` allowlist for packages with postinstall scripts (e.g. `better-sqlite3` prebuild-install). Without the allowlist, the build silently skips the postinstall and the package fails to load at runtime.
8	- `2026-04-23` — E2E tests invoke the CLI as `node dist/main.js <args>`, not `pnpm exec ax-next`. Shebang + `chmod +x` are present but tests use node directly to avoid pnpm bin-link flakiness.
9	- `2026-04-23` — Week 3 shipped as PR #2 (merged). Week 1–2 kernel shipped as PR #1.
10	- `2026-04-29` — Claude Agent SDK 0.2.119 encodes the project-dir name as `realpath(cwd)` with `/` → `-`, NOT the literal cwd string. On macOS `/var/...` becomes `-private-var-...`. Don't reconstruct the path; glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl`.
11	- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 with `settingSources: []` still writes outside `<HOME>/.claude/projects/`: `<HOME>/.claude.json` (~20KB cache: GrowthBook flags, anon userID, migrationVersion, model-cost cache), `<HOME>/.claude/backups/.claude.json.backup.<ts>` (accumulates per spawn), `<HOME>/.claude/policy-limits.json`. None are secrets, but workspace persistence should ignore everything outside `.claude/projects/`.
12	- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 jsonl entry types include non-turn records: `queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment` (e.g. `skill_listing` injected by the SDK even with `settingSources: []`). A `runner:read-transcript` parser must whitelist `user`/`assistant`/`tool`, not blacklist.
13	- `2026-04-29` — ax-next has no production data and never will need a schema migration that preserves rows. Existing `_v1` table suffixes (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`) are stable identifiers, NOT version pointers. ALTER TABLE in place is correct for every schema change; no v1→v2 split, ever. (See decisions.md 2026-04-29 entry.)
14	- `2026-05-03` — Host-pod listener layout (issue #39, PR #40): `@ax/ipc-http` on containerPort 8080 (runner-pod back-channel; Service :80) AND `@ax/http-server` on containerPort 9090 (public — `/chat`, `/health`, `/admin/*`, `/auth/*`, `/api/chat/*`; Service :9090). `serve` registers `/chat` + `/health` against http-server via `http:register-route`; no third listener. Chart values: `host.ports.ipc` / `host.ports.publicHttp` / `host.ipcServicePort` / `host.publicHttpServicePort`.
15	- `2026-05-03` — POST `/chat` (and any other state-changing route on the public surface) is gated by `@ax/http-server`'s CSRF subscriber. Headless callers must send `X-Requested-With: ax-admin` OR an Origin in `AX_HTTP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`. The chart sets `AX_HTTP_ALLOW_NO_ORIGINS=1` to silence the empty-allow-list warning when no Origin allow-list is configured.

file.content:
# Project context

Confirmed facts about ax-next. Not opinions.

- `2026-04-23` — `@ax/cli` is the only package allowed to import sibling plugins; enforced by `eslint.config.mjs` exception. All other inter-plugin communication goes through the hook bus.
- `2026-04-23` — Storage hook payload contract is `{ key: string, value: Uint8Array }` — opaque bytes, no backend vocab. Any `table`/`bucket`/`rowid` field would leak.
- `2026-04-23` — pnpm 10 requires `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` allowlist for packages with postinstall scripts (e.g. `better-sqlite3` prebuild-install). Without the allowlist, the build silently skips the postinstall and the package fails to load at runtime.
- `2026-04-23` — E2E tests invoke the CLI as `node dist/main.js <args>`, not `pnpm exec ax-next`. Shebang + `chmod +x` are present but tests use node directly to avoid pnpm bin-link flakiness.
- `2026-04-23` — Week 3 shipped as PR #2 (merged). Week 1–2 kernel shipped as PR #1.
- `2026-04-29` — Claude Agent SDK 0.2.119 encodes the project-dir name as `realpath(cwd)` with `/` → `-`, NOT the literal cwd string. On macOS `/var/...` becomes `-private-var-...`. Don't reconstruct the path; glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl`.
- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 with `settingSources: []` still writes outside `<HOME>/.claude/projects/`: `<HOME>/.claude.json` (~20KB cache: GrowthBook flags, anon userID, migrationVersion, model-cost cache), `<HOME>/.claude/backups/.claude.json.backup.<ts>` (accumulates per spawn), `<HOME>/.claude/policy-limits.json`. None are secrets, but workspace persistence should ignore everything outside `.claude/projects/`.
- `2026-04-29` — SDK 0.2.119 jsonl entry types include non-turn records: `queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment` (e.g. `skill_listing` injected by the SDK even with `settingSources: []`). A `runner:read-transcript` parser must whitelist `user`/`assistant`/`tool`, not blacklist.
- `2026-04-29` — ax-next has no production data and never will need a schema migration that preserves rows. Existing `_v1` table suffixes (`conversations_v1_*`, `audit_v1_*`) are stable identifiers, NOT version pointers. ALTER TABLE in place is correct for every schema change; no v1→v2 split, ever. (See decisions.md 2026-04-29 entry.)
- `2026-05-03` — Host-pod listener layout (issue #39, PR #40): `@ax/ipc-http` on containerPort 8080 (runner-pod back-channel; Service :80) AND `@ax/http-server` on containerPort 9090 (public — `/chat`, `/health`, `/admin/*`, `/auth/*`, `/api/chat/*`; Service :9090). `serve` registers `/chat` + `/health` against http-server via `http:register-route`; no third listener. Chart values: `host.ports.ipc` / `host.ports.publicHttp` / `host.ipcServicePort` / `host.publicHttpServicePort`.
- `2026-05-03` — POST `/chat` (and any other state-changing route on the public surface) is gated by `@ax/http-server`'s CSRF subscriber. Headless callers must send `X-Requested-With: ax-admin` OR an Origin in `AX_HTTP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`. The chart sets `AX_HTTP_ALLOW_NO_ORIGINS=1` to silence the empty-allow-list warning when no Origin allow-list is configured.

content.content:
1	# Patterns
2	
3	## Patterns (do these)
4	
5	- `2026-04-23` — When adding a new service hook, fill in the boundary-review block in the PR description (alternate impl / leaky field names / subscriber risk / wire surface). If you can't name an alternate impl, it probably shouldn't be a hook yet.
6	- `2026-04-23` — When scaffolding a plugin that touches fs/net/process/untrusted input, drop a `SECURITY.md` in the package directory at scaffold time, not later. Week 3's `packages/storage-sqlite/SECURITY.md` is the template.
7	- `2026-04-23` — For ESM "am I the main module?" checks, use `import { pathToFileURL } from 'node:url'` and compare `import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href`. `require.main === module` doesn't exist under ESM.
8	- `2026-04-29` — When reading a Claude Agent SDK jsonl by sessionId, glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` rather than reconstructing the encoded-cwd directory. The SDK encodes `realpath(cwd)`, not the literal cwd, so any symlink (e.g. macOS `/var` → `/private/var`) breaks reconstruction. SessionId is unique across project dirs.
9	- `2026-04-29` — When parsing SDK jsonl, whitelist turn-bearing entry types (`user`/`assistant`/`tool`) rather than blacklisting bookkeeping types. The SDK adds new bookkeeping kinds (`queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment`) over time; a blacklist drifts.
10	- `2026-05-03` — When a PR wires a new plugin into the k8s preset, the chart-shape test (`deploy/charts/ax-next/__tests__/env-shape.test.ts`) is the contract that catches missed env-var stamping. The test scans `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `env.*` reads and asserts both directions against the rendered deployment. Run it; if it fails, the chart's env block is incomplete — fix in the same PR.
11	- `2026-05-03` — Subcommands that need an HTTP front door should register routes via `http:register-route` against `@ax/http-server`, not spin up their own `http.createServer`. Mirror the channel-web / auth-oidc / teams pattern: a plugin with `manifest.calls: ['http:register-route']`, routes registered in `init()`. Multiple listeners on the same pod cause `EADDRINUSE` collisions and bypass the http-server's CSRF / body-cap subscribers.
12	
13	## Anti-Patterns (don't do these)
14	
15	- `2026-04-23` — Don't put backend-specific field names in hook payloads (`sha`, `bucket`, `pod_name`, `table`, `rowid`). If the name only makes sense for one impl, rename before subscribers depend on it.
16	- `2026-04-23` — Don't land "we'll wire it later" infrastructure. Either a live caller reaches it in the same PR or it doesn't merge. Symmetric API pairs (`get`/`set`) with a documented near-term consumer are the narrow exception.
17

# Patterns

## Patterns (do these)

- `2026-04-23` — When adding a new service hook, fill in the boundary-review block in the PR description (alternate impl / leaky field names / subscriber risk / wire surface). If you can't name an alternate impl, it probably shouldn't be a hook yet.
- `2026-04-23` — When scaffolding a plugin that touches fs/net/process/untrusted input, drop a `SECURITY.md` in the package directory at scaffold time, not later. Week 3's `packages/storage-sqlite/SECURITY.md` is the template.
- `2026-04-23` — For ESM "am I the main module?" checks, use `import { pathToFileURL } from 'node:url'` and compare `import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href`. `require.main === module` doesn't exist under ESM.
- `2026-04-29` — When reading a Claude Agent SDK jsonl by sessionId, glob `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` rather than reconstructing the encoded-cwd directory. The SDK encodes `realpath(cwd)`, not the literal cwd, so any symlink (e.g. macOS `/var` → `/private/var`) breaks reconstruction. SessionId is unique across project dirs.
- `2026-04-29` — When parsing SDK jsonl, whitelist turn-bearing entry types (`user`/`assistant`/`tool`) rather than blacklisting bookkeeping types. The SDK adds new bookkeeping kinds (`queue-operation`, `last-prompt`, `attachment`) over time; a blacklist drifts.
- `2026-05-03` — When a PR wires a new plugin into the k8s preset, the chart-shape test (`deploy/charts/ax-next/__tests__/env-shape.test.ts`) is the contract that catches missed env-var stamping. The test scans `presets/k8s/src/index.ts` for `env.*` reads and asserts both directions against the rendered deployment. Run it; if it fails, the chart's env block is incomplete — fix in the same PR.
- `2026-05-03` — Subcommands that need an HTTP front door should register routes via `http:register-route` against `@ax/http-server`, not spin up their own `http.createServer`. Mirror the channel-web / auth-oidc / teams pattern: a plugin with `manifest.calls: ['http:register-route']`, routes registered in `init()`. Multiple listeners on the same pod cause `EADDRINUSE` collisions and bypass the http-server's CSRF / body-cap subscribers.

## Anti-Patterns (don't do these)

- `2026-04-23` — Don't put backend-specific field names in hook payloads (`sha`, `bucket`, `pod_name`, `table`, `rowid`). If the name only makes sense for one impl, rename before subscribers depend on it.
- `2026-04-23` — Don't land "we'll wire it later" infrastructure. Either a live caller reaches it in the same PR or it doesn't merge. Symmetric API pairs (`get`/`set`) with a documented near-term consumer are the narrow exception.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/vpulim/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.1.0/skills/using-git-worktrees

# Using Git Worktrees

## Overview

Ensure work happens in an isolated workspace. Prefer your platform's native worktree tools. Fall back to manual git worktrees only when no native tool is available.

**Core principle:** Detect existing isolation first. Then use native tools. Then fall back to git. Never fight the harness.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace."

## Step 0: Detect Existing Isolation

**Before creating anything, check if you are already in an isolated workspace.**

```bash
GIT_DIR=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
GIT_COMMON=$(cd "$(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)" 2>/dev/null && pwd -P)
BRANCH=$(git branch --show-current)
```

**Submodule guard:** `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` is also true inside git submodules. Before concluding "already in a worktree," verify you are not in a submodule:

```bash
# If this returns a path, you're in a submodule, not a worktree — treat as normal repo
git rev-parse --show-superproject-working-tree 2>/dev/null
```

**If `GIT_DIR != GIT_COMMON` (and not a submodule):** You are already in a linked worktree. Skip to Step 3 (Project Setup). Do NOT create another worktree.

Report with branch state:
- On a branch: "Already in isolated workspace at `<path>` on branch `<name>`."
- Detached HEAD: "Already in isolated workspace at `<path>` (detached HEAD, externally managed). Branch creation needed at finish time."

**If `GIT_DIR == GIT_COMMON` (or in a submodule):** You are in a normal repo checkout.

Has the user already indicated their worktree preference in your instructions? If not, ask for consent before creating a worktree:

> "Would you like me to set up an isolated worktree? It protects your current branch from changes."

Honor any existing declared preference without asking. If the user declines consent, work in place and skip to Step 3.

## Step 1: Create Isolated Workspace

**You have two mechanisms. Try them in this order.**

### 1a. Native Worktree Tools (preferred)

The user has asked for an isolated workspace (Step 0 consent). Do you already have a way to create a worktree? It might be a tool with a name like `EnterWorktree`, `WorktreeCreate`, a `/worktree` command, or a `--worktree` flag. If you do, use it and skip to Step 3.

Native tools handle directory placement, branch creation, and cleanup automatically. Using `git worktree add` when you have a native tool creates phantom state your harness can't see or manage.

Only proceed to Step 1b if you have no native worktree tool available.

### 1b. Git Worktree Fallback

**Only use this if Step 1a does not apply** — you have no native worktree tool available. Create a worktree manually using git.

#### Directory Selection

Follow this priority order. Explicit user preference always beats observed filesystem state.

1. **Check your instructions for a declared worktree directory preference.** If the user has already specified one, use it without asking.

2. **Check for an existing project-local worktree directory:**
   ```bash
   ls -d .worktrees 2>/dev/null     # Preferred (hidden)
   ls -d worktrees 2>/dev/null      # Alternative
   ```
   If found, use it. If both exist, `.worktrees` wins.

3. **Check for an existing global directory:**
   ```bash
   project=$(basename "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")
   ls -d ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/$project 2>/dev/null
   ```
   If found, use it (backward compatibility with legacy global path).

4. **If there is no other guidance available**, default to `.worktrees/` at the project root.

#### Safety Verification (project-local directories only)

**MUST verify directory is ignored before creating worktree:**

```bash
git check-ignore -q .worktrees 2>/dev/null || git check-ignore -q worktrees 2>/dev/null
```

**If NOT ignored:** Add to .gitignore, commit the change, then proceed.

**Why critical:** Prevents accidentally committing worktree contents to repository.

Global directories (`~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/`) need no verification.

#### Create the Worktree

```bash
project=$(basename "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")

# Determine path based on chosen location
# For project-local: path="$LOCATION/$BRANCH_NAME"
# For global: path="~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/$project/$BRANCH_NAME"

git worktree add "$path" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
cd "$path"
```

**Sandbox fallback:** If `git worktree add` fails with a permission error (sandbox denial), tell the user the sandbox blocked worktree creation and you're working in the current directory instead. Then run setup and baseline tests in place.

## Step 3: Project Setup

Auto-detect and run appropriate setup:

```bash
# Node.js
if [ -f package.json ]; then npm install; fi

# Rust
if [ -f Cargo.toml ]; then cargo build; fi

# Python
if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
if [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then poetry install; fi

# Go
if [ -f go.mod ]; then go mod download; fi
```

## Step 4: Verify Clean Baseline

Run tests to ensure workspace starts clean:

```bash
# Use project-appropriate command
npm test / cargo test / pytest / go test ./...
```

**If tests fail:** Report failures, ask whether to proceed or investigate.

**If tests pass:** Report ready.

### Report

```
Worktree ready at <full-path>
Tests passing (<N> tests, 0 failures)
Ready to implement <feature-name>
```

## Quick Reference

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Already in linked worktree | Skip creation (Step 0) |
| In a submodule | Treat as normal repo (Step 0 guard) |
| Native worktree tool available | Use it (Step 1a) |
| No native tool | Git worktree fallback (Step 1b) |
| `.worktrees/` exists | Use it (verify ignored) |
| `worktrees/` exists | Use it (verify ignored) |
| Both exist | Use `.worktrees/` |
| Neither exists | Check instruction file, then default `.worktrees/` |
| Global path exists | Use it (backward compat) |
| Directory not ignored | Add to .gitignore + commit |
| Permission error on create | Sandbox fallback, work in place |
| Tests fail during baseline | Report failures + ask |
| No package.json/Cargo.toml | Skip dependency install |

## Common Mistakes

### Fighting the harness

- **Problem:** Using `git worktree add` when the platform already provides isolation
- **Fix:** Step 0 detects existing isolation. Step 1a defers to native tools.

### Skipping detection

- **Problem:** Creating a nested worktree inside an existing one
- **Fix:** Always run Step 0 before creating anything

### Skipping ignore verification

- **Problem:** Worktree contents get tracked, pollute git status
- **Fix:** Always use `git check-ignore` before creating project-local worktree

### Assuming directory location

- **Problem:** Creates inconsistency, violates project conventions
- **Fix:** Follow priority: existing > global legacy > instruction file > default

### Proceeding with failing tests

- **Problem:** Can't distinguish new bugs from pre-existing issues
- **Fix:** Report failures, get explicit permission to proceed

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Create a worktree when Step 0 detects existing isolation
- Use `git worktree add` when you have a native worktree tool (e.g., `EnterWorktree`). This is the #1 mistake — if you have it, use it.
- Skip Step 1a by jumping straight to Step 1b's git commands
- Create worktree without verifying it's ignored (project-local)
- Skip baseline test verification
- Proceed with failing tests without asking

**Always:**
- Run Step 0 detection first
- Prefer native tools over git fallback
- Follow directory priority: existing > global legacy > instruction file > default
- Verify directory is ignored for project-local
- Auto-detect and run project setup
- Verify clean test baseline

I have local unpushed commits (docs only) on `main` ahead of `origin/main`. The handoff and prior patterns say branch off `origin/main`. I have a native `EnterWorktree` tool — let me load its schema (the skill prefers native tools over manual `git worktree`).

`EnterWorktree` with the default `fresh` base ref branches from `origin/main` — exactly what the handoff wants. The handoff explicitly mandates a worktree, satisfying the tool's usage requirement. Creating it now.

Now **Phase 1/2 — Brainstorm + Design**. The handoff is already a detailed plan; my job is to ground its claims against real code (decision 24 lesson: verify, don't trust signatures) and make the scope call on F2a. input.description: Investigate F2a runner crash on resume
input.prompt:
I'm investigating a runner-side bug (call it "F2a") in the ax-next repo. Context: when a serving runner pod is killed mid-turn, the conversation's last assistant turn is interrupted mid-stream (the transcript jsonl ends with a partial/truncated assistant turn). When the user clicks "retry", a FRESH runner pod is spawned that re-runs the last user turn against the existing conversation by resuming the SDK session. The fresh runner appears to crash with exit code 1 ~2 seconds after it becomes ready — likely while resuming/replaying that interrupted transcript.

Your job is READ-ONLY investigation. Do NOT edit anything. I need a precise map of the runner's resume/replay code path so I can decide whether the root cause is obvious and the fix is small (fold into current PR) or needs deeper work (split into a separate follow-up).

Search breadth: very thorough — look across the whole `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/` package and any adjacent transcript/session code.

Specifically find and report:

1. **The resume entry point.** Where does the runner decide to resume an existing SDK session vs start fresh? Look for `query({ resume })` or `resume:` or `resumeSessionId` or env vars like `AX_RESUME` / `AX_SESSION_ID`. Quote the exact code (file:line) that constructs the SDK `query()` options including any `resume` field.

2. **How the transcript jsonl is located/loaded on resume.** Under runner-owned-sessions (PRs #125/#127/#132), transcripts live in the runner's workspace as jsonl files under `<HOME>/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl`. Find the code that materializes / locates / reads that jsonl before resume. Is there any validation, parsing, or truncation handling? Quote it.

3. **What happens to a partial/truncated last turn.** Is there ANY code that detects or repairs an interrupted last assistant turn (e.g. an assistant message with no terminal/result record, a dangling tool_use with no tool_result)? Or does it pass the raw jsonl straight to `query({resume})`?

4. **The crash surface.** Where would an exception during resume/query-init land? Is there a top-level try/catch in the runner main that would (a) catch it and POST a `chat-end` with a terminated outcome to the host before exiting, or (b) let it crash with exit 1 uncaught? Quote the runner's main() error handling and how/whether it POSTs `event.chat-end` on failure. This matters because the host-side fix (F2b) depends on whether the runner reports `chat-end` before dying.

5. **Any existing tests** in `agent-claude-sdk-runner` that exercise resume / `query({resume})` / interrupted-transcript handling.

Report back as a structured summary with exact file:line citations and short code quotes. Conclude with your assessment: is the root cause of an exit-1-on-resume-of-interrupted-transcript identifiable from the code, and would a fix be small/surgical or require significant work? Do not speculate beyond what the code shows — if the crash cause isn't determinable from static reading, say so.

content.text:
The Explore agent surfaced a **critical grounding fact**: the runner already catches resume exceptions and **always POSTs `event.chat-end` with a `terminated` outcome before exiting code 1** (`main.ts:1191-1201`). That confirms F2b's hypothesis #1 exactly — the runner reports a terminated `chat-end`, `onChatEnd` resolves the deferred, `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` stays true, the `fireTurnError` chokepoint is skipped, and the SSE hangs. So **F2b is a well-grounded safety net.**

It also confirms F2a's root cause is **not statically determinable** (it's inside the SDK's resume logic) — fixing it needs live cluster repro + image rebuild, a different package and verification loop. Let me grep the orchestrator to ground F2b precisely before finalizing the scope call.

content.text: Let me read the key orchestrator regions: `fireTurnError` definition, `onSessionTerminate`, the two `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` chokepoints, the early-spawn returns, and `onChatEnd`.

I now have F2b fully grounded. The reqId-restamping is confirmed: `onChatEnd` calls `resolveWaiterFor(ctx.reqId, ctx.sessionId, ...)` with a sessionId fallback precisely because "the IPC server stamps a fresh ctx.reqId per request" (orchestrator.ts:1552-1553). So firing `chat:turn-error` with `ctx.reqId` from `onChatEnd` would NOT match the SSE's original reqId — it must match by **conversationId**, mirroring the existing `chat:turn-end` done-frame subscriber (sse.ts:271-272).

The critical open question: does the IPC-fired `chat:end` ctx actually carry `conversationId`? The existing `chat:turn-end` subscriber relies on it, so by analogy it should — but I must verify, not assume. Let me trace how the IPC server stamps ctx for these events.

content.text:
Confirmed: the IPC listener stamps `conversationId` onto the ctx for runner-fired events (listener.ts:144-146), so the orchestrator's `onChatEnd` receives a ctx with `conversationId` set — the conversationId-match path for F2b is viable.

There's one architectural crux that determines the whole F2b design: **does the orchestrator's own `bus.fire('chat:end', ...)` (in the early-return and chokepoint paths) re-enter `onChatEnd`?** If `onChatEnd` is a `chat:end` bus subscriber, then adding turn-error logic there would automatically cover the early-spawn returns (making a separate explicit wire a *double*-fire). If it's a direct IPC-only callback, the handoff's additive design is exactly right. Let me read `plugin.ts` to settle this.

content.content:
1	import type { AgentOutcome, Plugin } from '@ax/core';
2	import {
3	  createOrchestrator,
4	  PLUGIN_NAME,
5	  type ChatOrchestratorConfig,
6	  type AgentInvokeInput,
7	} from './orchestrator.js';
8	
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	// @ax/chat-orchestrator plugin
11	//
12	// Registers `agent:invoke` — the host-side entrypoint a CLI (or any other host)
13	// uses to drive a single user→agent turn-sequence. Subscribes to `chat:end`
14	// so the orchestrator can capture the outcome the runner emits via its
15	// /event.chat-end IPC POST.
16	//
17	// Everything spicy happens inside `createOrchestrator`; this file is just
18	// the manifest + hook registration.
19	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20	
21	export function createChatOrchestratorPlugin(
22	  config: ChatOrchestratorConfig,
23	): Plugin {
24	  return {
25	    manifest: {
26	      name: PLUGIN_NAME,
27	      version: '0.0.0',
28	      registers: ['agent:invoke'],
29	      // The orchestrator drives the per-chat lifecycle by calling these
30	      // peers. `session:create` is NOT listed — sandbox:open-session mints
31	      // the session itself; a double-create throws duplicate-session. The
32	      // orchestrator intentionally delegates session minting to the sandbox
33	      // plugin. `ipc:stop` is NOT listed either: sandbox-subprocess stops
34	      // its own per-session listener on child-close (no host-side caller
35	      // needed), and the k8s preset uses @ax/ipc-http which has a
36	      // process-scoped listener with no per-session start/stop hooks.
37	      //
38	      // `agents:resolve` is a HARD dep as of Week 9.5 — every chat goes
39	      // through the ACL gate before the sandbox is spawned. This forces
40	      // any preset that wires the orchestrator to also wire @ax/agents,
41	      // which is the intended coupling.
42	      calls: [
43	        'session:queue-work',
44	        'session:terminate',
45	        'sandbox:open-session',
46	        'agents:resolve',
47	      ],
48	      // ----- conditionally-called peers (NOT in `calls`) -----
49	      //
50	      // Week 10–12 Task 16 (J6) introduces `conversations:get`,
51	      // `conversations:bind-session`, and `session:is-alive`. The
52	      // orchestrator dispatches these ONLY when `ctx.conversationId` is
53	      // set — i.e. on the channel-web path that ALSO loads
54	      // @ax/conversations + a session backend with the new hook. Plugins
55	      // outside that path (CLI canary, mcp-client e2e harness) drive the
56	      // orchestrator without a conversation context, so they don't need
57	      // the peers loaded.
58	      //
59	      // Phase 2B adds `system-prompt:augment` to this same category. The
60	      // hook is registered by @ax/memory-strata (auto-inject path) and any
61	      // future personalization / tenant-policy provider. Listing it in
62	      // `calls` would force every preset wiring the orchestrator to also
63	      // wire a provider — but the CLI canary, the single-tenant preset,
64	      // and any deploy that doesn't load memory-strata MUST stay functional
65	      // without one. The orchestrator gates with `bus.hasService(...)` and
66	      // no-ops when absent.
67	      //
68	      // We gate each call with `bus.hasService(...)` at runtime to keep
69	      // the orchestrator usable in those non-channel-web presets. Failing
70	      // closed (no routing, no bind) is the correct degraded behavior —
71	      // a missing conversations plugin can't keep state about active
72	      // sessions anyway.
73	      //
74	      // Same pattern as the existing `ipc:stop` non-declaration: when a
75	      // hook is conditionally consumed, we don't declare it.
76	      // We listen to `chat:end` to capture the outcome emitted by the
77	      // runner (via the IPC server's /event.chat-end handler). The
78	      // subscriber is a pass-through: resolves the waiting deferred,
79	      // returns undefined, doesn't veto and doesn't transform.
80	      //
81	      // We also listen to `chat:turn-end` so that in one-shot mode
82	      // (the default for 6.5a) we queue a `cancel` into the runner's
83	      // inbox after the first user message completes — the runner is
84	      // persistent by design, so without this signal it would block
85	      // forever on inbox.next() and the agent:invoke would time out.
86	      //
87	      // We listen to `session:terminate` (Fault A) so a sandbox that dies
88	      // mid-turn on the routed/warm path — which doesn't watch handle.exited
89	      // — surfaces a turn-error on the SSE promptly instead of hanging until
90	      // chatTimeoutMs. (session:terminate is ALSO in `calls` — the
91	      // orchestrator both requests teardown and observes the broadcast the
92	      // session store re-fires; the bus keeps the service + subscriber lanes
93	      // separate.)
94	      subscribes: ['chat:end', 'chat:turn-end', 'session:terminate'],
95	    },
96	    init({ bus }) {
97	      const orch = createOrchestrator(bus, config);
98	
99	      bus.registerService<AgentInvokeInput, AgentOutcome>(
100	        'agent:invoke',
101	        PLUGIN_NAME,
102	        async (ctx, input) => orch.runAgentInvoke(ctx, input),
103	      );
104	
105	      bus.subscribe<{ outcome: AgentOutcome }>(
106	        'chat:end',
107	        PLUGIN_NAME,
108	        async (ctx, payload) => {
109	          orch.onChatEnd(ctx, payload);
110	          return undefined; // pass-through; no transform, no veto.
111	        },
112	      );
113	
114	      bus.subscribe<{ reason?: string; reqId?: string }>(
115	        'chat:turn-end',
116	        PLUGIN_NAME,
117	        async (ctx, payload) => {
118	          orch.onTurnEnd(ctx, payload);
119	          return undefined;
120	        },
121	      );
122	
123	      bus.subscribe<{ sessionId?: string }>(
124	        'session:terminate',
125	        PLUGIN_NAME,
126	        async (ctx, payload) => {
127	          await orch.onSessionTerminate(ctx, payload);
128	          return undefined; // observation-only; never vetoes teardown.
129	        },
130	      );
131	    },
132	  };
133	}
134

content.text: **Decisive finding:** `onChatEnd` is registered as a **`chat:end` bus subscriber** (plugin.ts:105-112). So *every* `bus.fire('chat:end', ...)` — the runner-reported IPC path AND the orchestrator's own early-return/chokepoint fires — re-enters `onChatEnd`. This reshapes the F2b design and lets me avoid both double-fires and spurious fires with a single clean guard. Let me grep the `AgentOutcome` type and the bus reentrancy precedent to finalize.

input.description: Find AgentOutcome type and resolveWaiterFor usage

content.text:
`AgentOutcome = { kind: 'complete'; messages } | { kind: 'terminated'; reason; error? }` — so `kind !== 'complete'` cleanly narrows to give `.reason`. `resolveWaiterFor` is called at onChatEnd (1500) and onTurnEnd (1539). 

The design is now clear. The key refinement the architecture enables: **`onChatEnd` runs for every `chat:end` fire, so I make `resolveWaiterFor` return whether it resolved a *live* (unsettled) waiter — a `wasInFlight` guard that fires turn-error only for the runner-reported live-crash path, while the chokepoint paths (deferred already settled) and reaped-warm-runner late chat:ends (waiter gone) skip it — no double-fires, no spurious logs.**

Let me read the agent-status-store and the existing F1 test to confirm `show()` properly transitions error→working.

`agentStatusActions.show('Thinking…')` sets `{ mode: 'working', text, retry: null, dismiss: null }` — so calling it when `mode==='error'` cleanly transitions to working AND clears the retry/dismiss handlers and error styling. So F1's fix (broaden the mount gate from `=== 'hidden'` to `!== 'working'`) is exactly right. The existing tests don't mount `RunningEffect` (stub runtime reports `running=false`), so to test it directly I'll export `RunningEffect`.

Let me look at the existing orchestrator and sse test harnesses to ground the F2b tests, then write the plan.

content.text: Let me read the existing SSE turn-error test and the orchestrator test harness to ground my new test cases.

input.command: echo "=== sse.test.ts: turn-error block ===" && grep -n "turn-error\|turnError\|conversationId\|reqId\|chat:turn-error\|describe\|it(" packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/server/sse.test.ts | head -60

content.text: I need to check the existing negative test (`turn-error with a non-matching reqId does not close us` at line 460): it fires with `initCtx` + reqId `r-other`. If `initCtx.conversationId` happens to equal the resolved `conversationId` (`cnv_test`), my "match reqId OR conversationId" change would suddenly make that test match-and-close, breaking it. I must check what `initCtx` carries.

`initCtx` has **no conversationId** (bootHandler:138-142), so the existing negative turn-error test stays green under my "reqId OR conversationId" change (both match conditions are false). 

I now have everything grounded. Let me look at the orchestrator test harness to ground the F2b orchestrator tests, then I'll write the plan + log decisions.

input.command: echo "=== orchestrator.test.ts: harness + fired-event capture ===" && grep -n "describe\|function makeOrch\|function boot\|createOrchestrator\|onChatEnd\|fireSpy\|firedEvents\|bus.fire\|chat:turn-error\|fired\b\|registerService\|queue-work\|sandbox:open\|reqId:" packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts | head -70

content.text: The orchestrator test already has a Fault A turn-error section (lines 409-615) with a `turnErrorCtx` helper and tests for the chokepoints, session:terminate, and the completed-turn negative. Let me read that section to mirror its harness for the new F2b tests.

I have the full Fault A test harness. The existing `'a completed turn does NOT fire chat:turn-error'` test fires `chat:end` with `reqId: originatingReqId` (matching) + `kind: 'complete'` — under my change `wasInFlight=true` but complete → no fire, so it stays green. Let me read `buildMocks` and the harness top to understand sessionId flow for my new F2b tests.
I now have everything fully grounded. Let me record the design decisions (to the **main checkout's** canonical `decisions.md`), write the plan doc, and set up task tracking — in one batch.

input.old_string: | 2026-05-23 | Implement **inline, strict TDD** (test→red→impl→green per area), NOT per-task subagents | Full map already held (all fire sites, frame/event shapes, AI-SDK error-chunk mechanism); the diff is subtle-dense — reqId-vs-conversationId match, `!deferred.settled` guard, error-chunk-vs-finish — exactly where subagents diverge. Inline gives me direct control of each subtlety; ship loop is where lean context matters and the impl is small enough to stay under cap. Mirrors decisions 19/38/50. | Per-task subagents (rejected here: high prompt-overhead to re-convey the subtleties + divergence risk on the matching/guard decisions) |

| 2026-05-24 | Fault A F1+F2b yolo-ship: isolated worktree (`EnterWorktree` → `worktree-fault-a-followups-f1-f2b`, fresh off origin/main); decisions.md written to the **main checkout** | Same as decisions 40/47/52 — handoff mandated a worktree + [[feedback_shared_checkout_use_worktree]] (concurrent agents switch branches mid-task). Worktree needed a full `pnpm install` (node_modules absent in a fresh worktree); plan doc lives under the gitignored `docs/plans/`. | Work in-place (rejected: shared checkout); decisions in worktree (rejected: fragments memory) |
| 2026-05-24 | F2a (runner exit-1 on resume-of-interrupted-transcript) **SPLIT to a documented follow-up** (TODO.md + memory), NOT folded into this PR; F1+F2b ship together | Explore confirmed the runner ALREADY catches the resume exception and POSTs `event.chat-end{terminated}` before exit 1 (main.ts:1191-1201) — so F2a's root cause is INSIDE the SDK's `query({resume})` reading a truncated jsonl, NOT statically determinable. Fixing needs live cluster repro + an image-rebuild loop ([[feedback_docker_build_cache_runner_fixes]]) + a different package (agent-claude-sdk-runner). F2b is the safety net that surfaces the user-visible stuck-"Starting sandbox…" regardless. A speculative jsonl-repair fix without a repro violates systematic-debugging + Bug Fix Policy (no meaningful regression test without the failure mode). | Fold F2a in (rejected: different package + verify loop, speculative fix, blows scope+context); silently drop (rejected: handoff forbids) |
| 2026-05-24 | F2b core insight: `onChatEnd` is a **`chat:end` subscriber** (plugin.ts:105), so EVERY `bus.fire('chat:end')` — runner-reported IPC + orchestrator early-returns + chokepoints — re-enters it. Surface the turn-error IN `onChatEnd`, guarded by a new `wasInFlight` return from `resolveWaiterFor` (true iff it resolved a previously-UNSETTLED waiter) AND `outcome.kind !== 'complete'` | `wasInFlight` cleanly separates three cases without double/spurious fires: (a) runner-reported live crash → waiter unsettled → fire (matched by conversationId, reqId restamped); (b) chokepoint paths → deferred already settled before their `bus.fire('chat:end')` → onChatEnd skips, their OWN explicit fireTurnError (kept from #137) is the single fire; (c) reaped-warm-runner late terminated chat:end after a completed turn → waiter gone → no spurious log/event. | Centralize ALL turn-error firing in onChatEnd + remove the chokepoint fires (rejected: spurious `chat_turn_error` logs when a reaped warm runner POSTs a late terminated chat:end after a success); fire unconditionally on kind!=='complete' (rejected: double-fires at chokepoints + spurious at reaped runners) |
| 2026-05-24 | onChatEnd-fired turn-error matched at the SSE by `ctx.conversationId === conversationId` (OR the existing reqId match); early-spawn returns (3 sites: routed+fresh `queue-work-failed`, fresh `sandbox-open-failed`) get explicit `fireTurnError` matched by **reqId** | The IPC listener restamps `ctx.reqId` per request but stamps `ctx.conversationId` (listener.ts:144) — so the runner-reported path can only join the SSE by conversationId (mirrors the existing done-frame `chat:turn-end` subscriber, sse.ts:271). The early-returns `unregisterWaiter` BEFORE firing chat:end → onChatEnd's wasInFlight=false skips them → they need their own explicit fire, and still hold the original `ctx.reqId` so reqId-match works. `initCtx` in sse.test has no conversationId so the existing reqId-mismatch negative test stays green. | Match the onChatEnd path by reqId (rejected: restamped, never matches); rely on onChatEnd for early-returns too (rejected: waiter unregistered first → wasInFlight=false → wouldn't fire) |
| 2026-05-24 | Implement F1+F2b **INLINE strict TDD**, not per-task subagents; **export `RunningEffect`** for direct mount/unmount testing | ~5 surgical files across 2 packages, fully mapped; context well under cap; ship loop is where lean context matters. `RunningEffect` only mounts under `ThreadPrimitive.If running` (false in the stub runtime), so exporting it lets the test exercise the exact mount/unmount transitions the handoff specifies. Mirrors decisions 19/38/50/58. | Per-task subagents (rejected: re-deriving known context costs more, divergence risk on a tiny diff); drive RunningEffect via a running=true runtime (rejected: useLocalRuntime running-state hard to force synchronously) |
