# Fault A follow-ups F1 + F2b — impl plan

Source: `.claude/prompts/fault-a-followups-f1-f2.md`. Completes PR #137.
Worktree `worktree-fault-a-followups-f1-f2b` off origin/main. Inline strict TDD.

**Scope:** F1 (client) + F2b (host). **F2a SPLIT** to a documented follow-up
(runner-side, needs cluster repro + image rebuild; root cause is inside the SDK's
`query({resume})` per Explore — runner already POSTs terminated chat:end before
exit 1, main.ts:1191-1201). See decisions 60-64, TODO.md.

## Task 1 — F1: sticky error row (channel-web)
`packages/channel-web/src/components/AgentStatus.tsx`:
- Export `RunningEffect` (for direct mount/unmount test).
- Mount gate: `mode !== 'working'` (was `=== 'hidden'`) → `show('Thinking…')`. Resets a
  stale `error` → `working` on a new turn. (`show` clears retry/dismiss + error styling.)
- Unmount: UNCHANGED (hide only when `working`) — error set DURING a turn persists (#137).
Test `__tests__/agent-status.test.tsx` (RED first):
  (a) error mode + RunningEffect mounts → resets to working ('Thinking…', retry cleared, not error);
  (b) hidden→working on mount, then unmount → hides;
  (c) error set while mounted, then unmount → error PERSISTS (not hidden).

## Task 2 — F2b: surface turn-error on any terminated chat:end + early-spawn (chat-orchestrator + channel-web)
`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts`:
- `resolveWaiterFor` returns `boolean` — true iff it resolved a previously-UNSETTLED waiter.
- `onChatEnd` → async; `const wasInFlight = resolveWaiterFor(...)`;
  `if (wasInFlight && payload.outcome.kind !== 'complete') await fireTurnError(ctx, ctx.reqId, payload.outcome.reason)`.
  (Runner-reported path: restamped reqId, ctx.conversationId set → SSE matches by conversationId.)
- 3 early-return sites — add `await fireTurnError(ctx, ctx.reqId, outcome.reason)` before `bus.fire('chat:end')`:
  routed queue-work-failed (~833), fresh sandbox-open-failed (~1307), fresh queue-work-failed (~1371). (reqId-matched.)
- KEEP the 2 chokepoint explicit fires + onSessionTerminate (wasInFlight guard prevents double-fire).
- Interface `Orchestrator.onChatEnd` → `Promise<void>`.
`packages/chat-orchestrator/src/plugin.ts`: `await orch.onChatEnd(ctx, payload);`
`packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts`: turn-error subscriber matches
  `payload.reqId === reqId` OR `ctx.conversationId === conversationId`; update comment.
Tests (RED first):
- orchestrator.test.ts: runner-reported terminated chat:end (restamped reqId + conversationId on ctx, live waiter) → fires turn-error, fire-ctx carries conversationId; terminated chat:end with NO live waiter → does NOT fire (guards double/spurious); early-spawn sandbox-open-failed + queue-work-failed → fire by reqId; existing 'completed turn does NOT fire' stays green.
- sse.test.ts: turn-error matched by conversationId (reqId mismatch) → error frame + close; existing reqId match/non-match stay green.

## Gate
`pnpm build` + `pnpm -r run test` + eslint on changed files (scope to changed files).

## Boundary review
No new hooks (reuses `chat:turn-error` from #137). No payload shape change. SSE match
widening is internal to the subscriber. `onChatEnd` signature → async is an internal
plugin change. No security-checklist trigger (no sandbox/IPC/plugin-load/dep/untrusted-path change;
`reason` is host-orchestrator vocabulary, not model/tool output).

it('chat:end fires exactly once across all exit paths', async () => {

Keep unmount (hide only when working). Export RunningEffect. RED-first tests in agent-status.test.tsx: (a) error→working on mount, (b) working→hidden on unmount, (c) error set during turn persists on unmount.

RED-first tests in orchestrator.test.ts + sse.test.ts.

TODO.md
--- TODO.md exists; tail ---
## Memory-strata Phase 5+ (friction-driven; don't pre-schedule)

Listed here only so the triggers are easy to find. Per the roadmap, these stay dormant until their stated trigger fires.

- [ ] **Multi-tenant memory scoping** — when ax-next opens to multi-tenant beyond per-agent isolation.
- [ ] **Curator-as-patch pipeline** — when user-facing memory governance is requested OR bad-observation incidents surface.
- [ ] **Reranker (Level 6) re-spike** — only with new candidate model + production BM25 recall evidence.
- [ ] **Memory replay / time-travel queries** — when a user asks "what did the agent know on date X?"
- [ ] **Cross-agent memory sharing** — explicit user request only (likely requires the Curator pipeline first).
- [ ] **Bring-your-own embedding provider** — Phase 3 dropped vectors; reopen only if Level 7 is re-spiked and lands "IN".

## UI Bugs

- [x] ~~**Errors resume old session and a chat title bug**~~ — fixed (PR #125). BUG 2/3: `channel-web/runtime.tsx` only cleared `conversationRef` on null and never set it on sidebar-select, so selecting an old chat then sending POSTed `conversationId:null` → a new conversation (lost history). Now mirrors `activeSessionId` into the ref unconditionally. BUG 1: single-turn chats stayed "New Chat" because the runner jsonl syncs ~1s after `chat:turn-end`, so `conversations:get` is empty at the only title attempt — `@ax/conversation-titles` now falls back to the turn-end payload's assistant `contentBlocks`.

## Web tools (@ax/web-tools, PR for `feat/web-tools-impl`)

- [x] ~~**Fix the `memory-search` host-executor `call.input` bug.** `packages/memory-strata/src/tools/memory-search.ts` reads `input.query` (bare-input shape) instead of `call.input.query`. The host-execution contract passes the **full `ToolCall`** `{ id, name, input }` to the `tool:execute:<name>` hook (see `@ax/mcp-client` and the new `@ax/web-tools` executors), so `memory-search` reads `undefined` in production — masked only by a unit test that invokes the hook with the wrong shape. Out of scope for the web-tools PR; fix + correct the test together (Bug Fix Policy). Low-risk, ~5-line change.~~ — done (2026-05-22): fixed in `memory-search.ts` **and the two sibling host tools** that shared the identical bug + identical test-masking — `memory-read-section.ts` (`call.input.docId`) and `memory-note.ts` (`call.input.subject`/`content`/…). All three executors now read `call.input`; all three test suites corrected to pass the real `{id,name,input}` envelope via an `asToolCall` helper (verified RED→GREEN per Bug Fix Policy). `pnpm --filter @ax/memory-strata build` + 163 src unit tests + eslint all green.
- [x] ~~**Real-API MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk for web tools.** The new `deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md` "Web tools" scenario (search → extract → url-guard refusal) is unit/canary-covered only. Real end-to-end needs an org admin to enable Web Search in the Claude Console + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on the host. Walk it next time the `kind-ax-next-dev` cluster is up with a key.~~ — done (2026-05-22): walked headlessly against `kind-ax-next-dev` (host pod already had `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`; signed the live bootstrap session into `ax_auth_session`, POSTed to `/api/chat/messages`). **web_search** returned live data (Node.js 26.0.0 + dated source URLs → Web Search IS enabled in the Console for this key); **web_extract** returned real nodejs.org/about page content; **url-guard** — the IMDS prompt is independently safe (model self-refuses), and a follow-up `.internal` prompt got the model to actually invoke `web_extract`, exercising the host-side guard: tool returned `invalid-payload` and the host log shows `@ax/web-tools tool:execute:web_extract → "url not allowed (must be a public http(s) URL…)"` at `web-extract.js:32`.
- [x] ~~**Two unrelated fixes parked on `feat/web-tools` (NOT in the web-tools PR).** `b3c87929` (`fix(auth-better): thread AX_PUBLIC_BASE_URL into better-auth baseURL`) and `494b9e7c` (`fix(channel-web): symmetric vertical spacing around tool-group pill`) rode on the `feat/web-tools` branch but are unrelated to web tools, so they were dropped from `feat/web-tools-impl` (rebased `--onto origin/main`) to keep the PR scoped. They are preserved on the untouched `feat/web-tools` branch — ship them via their own PR(s) or cherry-pick.~~ — done (2026-05-21): rebased onto `origin/main` (post-#121) and pushed to `main` as `44087f24` (auth baseURL) + `3d24f597` (channel-web spacing).
- [ ] **Web-tools cost/usage metering + per-agent search-spend visibility.** YAGNI now (plan "Out of scope"). Add a usage-metering subscriber hook if operators need per-agent web-search spend (web search bills ~$10/1k). Trigger: an operator asks for the visibility.

## Credentialed CLI tools & git Basic-auth (PR for `worktree-credentialed-cli-and-git-auth`)

Sub-projects **B** (git Basic-auth substitution in the credential-proxy MITM path) + **D** (skill-declared `capabilities.packages` → registry auto-allowlist + `uv`/`uvx`/`python3` in the agent image). Design: `docs/plans/2026-05-22-credentialed-cli-tools-and-git-auth-design.md`. Shipped green + review-clean; deferred items:

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (B) — real `git clone` over Basic-auth on kind.** Install a skill declaring `allowedHosts: [gitlab.com]` + a `GITLAB_TOKEN` (`api-key`) credential slot, then have the agent run `git clone https://oauth2:$GITLAB_TOKEN@gitlab.com/<path>.git` and confirm the clone succeeds (the proxy decodes → substitutes → re-encodes the Basic header). Needs `kind-ax-next-dev` up + a real GitLab read token. Covered by unit + MITM integration tests; the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE walk (D) — skill-declared CLI through the proxy on kind.** Install a skill declaring `capabilities.packages.npm: ['@linear/cli']` + `allowedHosts: [api.linear.app]` + a `LINEAR_API_KEY` slot, and confirm the agent can `npx @linear/cli ...` (registry auto-allowlisted to `registry.npmjs.org`, tool fetched on demand, Bearer key substituted by the proxy). The agent **image** changed in this PR (D), so rebuild it (`--no-cache` or verify the compiled image per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]) before walking.
- [ ] **Digest-pin the agent image's pinned-by-tag deps.** `uv`/`uvx` is pinned to `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.11.16` (version tag, not digest). Fold a digest-pin into the existing `tini`/`ca-certificates` digest-pin follow-up so all image deps are content-addressed. Low-risk; do them together.
- [ ] **Per-package registry allowlisting (vs whole-registry).** D auto-allowlists the *entire* `registry.npmjs.org` / PyPI when an ecosystem is declared (design §5.7 — bounded by the admin-skill trust boundary + session allowlist + canary, acceptable for MVP). A later tightening could restrict egress to the specific declared package paths. Trigger: a tighter supply-chain posture is requested.
- [ ] **Body-split placeholder substitution across TCP segments (pre-existing, out of scope).** B's `RequestFramer` fixes placeholder substitution for placeholders split across TCP segments in the request **head**; a placeholder split across segments inside a request **body** is still handled only by the per-chunk verbatim path (unchanged from before B). Bearer/api-key creds live in the head, so this rarely bites. Fix only if a real body-credential case appears.
- [ ] **Cross-session CLI tool caching / pre-warming (design §5.6, deferred).** MVP is per-session ephemeral `npx`/`uvx` fetch (re-fetch latency per session). Add a cache/pre-warm step if the latency becomes a problem. YAGNI now.
- [ ] **Go toolchain support (design §2/§5.6, deferred).** `packages.go` is rejected with `unsupported-package-ecosystem`. The grammar is shaped to add it (remove from the unsupported set + union a registry host). Trigger: a skill needs a Go CLI AND the ~300 MB+ image weight is acceptable.

## Concurrent-writer re-sync follow-ups (F-1/F-2, PR for `worktree-f1-f2-resync-followups`)

Completes PR #132 (per-turn server-backend re-sync) so the "robust to ANY concurrent writer, ANY backend" guarantee fully holds. Spec: `.claude/prompts/implement-f1-f2-resync-followups.md` + `docs/plans/2026-05-23-chat-transcript-loss-fix-impl.md` §Follow-ups. Shipped green + review-clean; deferred:

- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE cluster re-walk (F-2, any backend).** Re-walk PR #132's repro on `kind-ax-next-dev`: new chat → npx turn → attachment-upload turn → reload → assert all turns present, no synthetic `Continue…` turn. F-2 is runner-side (the final/idle commit now re-syncs), so the fast hostPath loop won't cover it — **rebuild the agent image first** (per [[docker-build-cache-runner-fixes]]). Covered by unit + the runner final-commit regression test; the cluster walk is the end-to-end proof.
- [ ] **MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE for F-1 needs a single-replica deployment.** F-1 fixes the `@ax/workspace-git` (workspace-git-core) single-replica backend's apply-bundle re-sync. The standard `ax-next-dev` cluster runs the multi-replica `@ax/workspace-git-server` backend (where the mismatch surfaces at *export*, the PR #132 path), so it does NOT exercise F-1's apply-bundle catch. Walk F-1 end-to-end only on a deployment configured with the single-replica backend. Covered by the real-backend integration test (`workspace-commit-notify-core-resync.test.ts`).

## Fault A follow-ups (F1 sticky error row + F2b unsurfaced terminated end — PR for `worktree-fault-a-followups-f1-f2b`)

Completes PR #137 (Fault A: sandbox death mid-turn → error+retry). F1 (client) + F2b (host) shipped here. Handoff: `.claude/prompts/fault-a-followups-f1-f2.md`. Deferred:

- [ ] **F2a — runner exits 1 replaying an interrupted transcript on retry (runner-side, NOT in this PR).** When the serving pod is killed mid-turn, the conversation's last assistant turn is left truncated in the runner-owned jsonl. On retry, a fresh runner resumes via `query({ resume: sessionId })` (`agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:564-574`, `HOME`→workspace at :641) and crashes ~2s after pod_ready. **Investigation (static read, 2026-05-24):** the runner does NO validation/repair of the jsonl before resume — it hands the raw (possibly truncated) transcript to the SDK, which reads `$HOME/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` itself. The runner ALREADY catches the exception and POSTs `event.chat-end{kind:'terminated'}` before `exit 1` (`main.ts:1191-1201`), so the user-visible stuck-"Starting sandbox…" is the *host* not surfacing that terminated end — **fixed by F2b in this PR** (onChatEnd now fires `chat:turn-error` on a runner-reported terminated outcome). fix(channel-web): reset stale error row when a new turn starts (Fault A F1)

After #137 wired turn-error to agentStatusActions.error, the status row got
stuck: RunningEffect only did hidden→working on mount, so a stale 'error' mode
(set by a prior turn-error) never cleared — the red row + retry button persisted
through the retry turn AND every later turn / new session until a full page
reload. Broaden the mount gate from `mode === 'hidden'` to `mode !== 'working'`
so a new turn resets a stale error to "Thinking…" (show() also clears the retry
handler + error styling). Unmount is unchanged (hide only when 'working'), so an
error set DURING a turn still persists after it ends — that persistence is the
whole point of #137.

Export RunningEffect so a unit test can drive the mount/unmount transitions
directly (in the app it only mounts inside <ThreadPrimitive.If running>). Tests:
stale error→working on mount, working→hidden on unmount, error-during-turn
persists on unmount (RED-before per Bug Fix Policy).

fix(chat-orchestrator,channel-web): surface turn-error on any terminated end (Fault A F2b)

#137 fired chat:turn-error only when the orchestrator SYNTHESIZED a terminated
outcome (the !resolvedByChatEndSubscriber chokepoints + session:terminate). It
did NOT fire when the runner itself reported a terminated outcome via
event.chat-end (e.g. crashing on a resume of an interrupted transcript): the IPC
chat:end resolves the deferred → resolvedByChatEndSubscriber stays true → the
chokepoint is skipped, and no chat:turn-end fires → the SSE hangs on "Starting
sandbox…". The early-spawn returns (queue-work-failed, sandbox-open-failed) were
also unwired.

onChatEnd is a chat:end subscriber, so EVERY bus.fire('chat:end') re-enters it.
Surface the turn-error there, guarded by a new `wasInFlight` return from
resolveWaiterFor (true iff it resolved a previously-UNSETTLED waiter) AND
outcome.kind !== 'complete'. wasInFlight cleanly separates the cases without
double/spurious fires: the runner-reported live crash fires (waiter unsettled);
the chokepoint paths skip it (deferred already settled before their bus.fire —
their own explicit fireTurnError stays the single fire); a reaped warm runner's
late terminated chat:end after a completed turn skips it (no live waiter).

The IPC server RESTAMPS ctx.reqId per request but stamps ctx.conversationId, so
the SSE turn-error subscriber now matches by reqId OR conversationId (mirroring
the done-frame chat:turn-end subscriber). The 3 early-spawn returns get an
explicit fireTurnError (they hold the original reqId; they unregister the waiter
before chat:end so onChatEnd skips them).

Tests (RED-before): orchestrator — runner-reported terminated chat:end fires
turn-error carrying conversationId; no-live-waiter and complete-with-live-waiter
do NOT fire; early-spawn sandbox-open-failed/queue-work-failed fire by reqId. sse
— turn-error matched by conversationId (restamped reqId) emits the error frame +
closes; different-conversation+reqId does not.

F2a (the runner exit-1 on resume-of-interrupted-transcript) is a separate
runner-side bug needing cluster repro + an image rebuild — documented in TODO.md;
F2b is the host-side safety net that surfaces it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head worktree-fault-a-followups-f1-f2b --title "fix(Fault A): F1 sticky error row + F2b surface runner-reported/early-spawn terminated end" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
Completes the Fault A error-surfacing work from #137. Two cohesive fixes; F2a (a runner-side resume crash) is split to a documented follow-up.

## F1 — sticky error row (client regression from #137)
Once `agentStatusActions` entered error mode it never reset on a later turn. `RunningEffect` (`AgentStatus.tsx`) only did `hidden→working` on mount, so a stale `'error'` mode (set by a prior turn-error) persisted — the red row + retry button stuck through the retry turn AND every subsequent turn / new session until a full page reload (it's in-memory module state). `agentStatusActions.error` was dead in prod before #137, so this only surfaced once #137 wired `onError` to it.

**Fix:** broaden the mount gate from `mode === 'hidden'` to `mode !== 'working'` so a new turn resets a stale error to `"Thinking…"` (`show()` also clears the retry handler + error styling). Unmount is unchanged (hide only when `'working'`), so an error set *during* a turn still persists after it ends — that persistence is the whole point of #137.

## F2b — surface turn-error on ANY terminated end
#137 fired `chat:turn-error` only when the orchestrator *synthesized* the terminated outcome (the `!resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` chokepoints + the `session:terminate` subscriber). It did **not** fire when:
1. **The runner reports a terminated outcome via `event.chat-end`** (the most likely cause of the walk's stuck "Starting sandbox…"): the IPC `chat:end` resolves the deferred → `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` stays `true` → the chokepoint is skipped, and no `chat:turn-end` fires → the SSE hangs.
2. **Early-spawn terminated returns** — `queue-work-failed`, `sandbox-open-failed`.

**Fix:** `onChatEnd` is a `chat:end` subscriber, so *every* `bus.fire('chat:end')` re-enters it. Surface the turn-error there, guarded by a new `wasInFlight` return from `resolveWaiterFor` (true iff it resolved a previously-**unsettled** waiter) **and** `outcome.kind !== 'complete'`. `wasInFlight` cleanly separates the cases without double/spurious fires:
- runner-reported live crash → waiter unsettled → **fires** (matched by conversationId);
- chokepoint paths → deferred already settled before their `bus.fire('chat:end')` → onChatEnd skips, their own explicit `fireTurnError` (kept from #137) is the single fire;
- reaped warm runner's late terminated `chat:end` after a completed turn → no live waiter → no spurious log/event.

The IPC server **restamps `ctx.reqId`** per request but stamps `ctx.conversationId`, so the SSE `chat:turn-error` subscriber now matches by **reqId OR conversationId** (mirroring the done-frame `chat:turn-end` subscriber). The 3 early-spawn returns get an explicit `fireTurnError` (they hold the original reqId; they `unregisterWaiter` before `chat:end`, so onChatEnd skips them).

## F2a — split to a follow-up (runner-side; see TODO.md)
On retry the fresh runner crashes (`exit 1`) resuming the interrupted transcript. Static investigation: the runner already catches the resume exception and POSTs `event.chat-end{terminated}` before exiting (`agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts:1191-1201`) — so the *user-visible* stuck state is the **host** not surfacing that terminated end, which **F2b fixes**. The crash root cause is inside the SDK's `query({resume})` reading a truncated jsonl, isn't statically determinable, and needs a cluster repro + image-rebuild loop in a different package. Documented in `TODO.md`; F2b is the safety net for any runner-reported abnormal end regardless.

## Tests (RED-before per Bug Fix Policy)
- **agent-status**: stale error→working on mount; working→hidden on unmount; error-during-turn persists on unmount.
- **orchestrator**: runner-reported terminated `chat:end` (restamped reqId) fires turn-error carrying conversationId; no-live-waiter and complete-with-live-waiter do **not** fire; early-spawn sandbox-open-failed / queue-work-failed fire by reqId.
- **sse**: turn-error matched by conversationId (restamped reqId) emits the error frame + closes; different-conversation + non-matching reqId does not.

## Gate
`pnpm build` (tsc clean) · `pnpm -r run test` (all 56 packages green, incl. chat-orchestrator 71 / channel-web 514) · eslint clean on changed files.

## Boundary review
No new hooks (reuses `chat:turn-error` from #137); no hook payload shape change. The SSE match-widening (reqId → reqId-OR-conversationId) is internal to the per-connection subscriber. `onChatEnd`'s signature → `Promise<void>` is an internal plugin change (only caller is the plugin's own `chat:end` subscriber). No service-hook signature change → no boundary-review entries required.

## Security note
No security-checklist trigger: no sandbox boundary / IPC transport / plugin-loading / dependency / caller-provided-path change. `reason` is host-orchestrator vocabulary (a fixed code set: `sandbox-exit-before-chat-end`, `chat-run-timeout`, `queue-work-failed`, …) or the runner-reported `outcome.reason`, which is validated by `EventChatEndSchema` at the IPC boundary and rendered as escaped React text in the error frame (no XSS).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

---
name: project_fault_a_f1_f2b_pr138
description: Fault A follow-ups F1 (sticky error row) + F2b (surface runner-reported/early-spawn terminated end) shipped as PR #138
metadata:
  type: project
---

2026-05-24 yolo-ship of `.claude/prompts/fault-a-followups-f1-f2.md` — completes
PR #137 (see [[project_fault_a_sse_turn_error_pr137]]). Isolated worktree
`worktree-fault-a-followups-f1-f2b` off origin/main. **PR #138**, base main.

**F1 (client, channel-web):** `RunningEffect` (AgentStatus.tsx) only did
hidden→working on mount, so a stale `'error'` row (from #137's turn-error →
`agentStatusActions.error`) never reset — stuck through the retry turn + every
later turn/new-session until a full page reload. Fix = broaden the mount gate
`mode === 'hidden'` → `mode !== 'working'` (`show()` clears retry + error styling);
unmount unchanged (hide only when 'working') so an error set DURING a turn still
persists (#137's point). Exported `RunningEffect` for direct mount/unmount tests.

**F2b (host, chat-orchestrator + channel-web):** #137 only fired `chat:turn-error`
when the orchestrator SYNTHESIZED the terminated outcome (chokepoints +
session:terminate). It missed the **runner-reported** terminated `event.chat-end`
(IPC resolves the deferred → `resolvedByChatEndSubscriber` stays true → chokepoint
skipped → no turn-end → SSE hangs on "Starting sandbox…") and the early-spawn
returns. **Key insight:** `onChatEnd` is a `chat:end` SUBSCRIBER (plugin.ts:105),
so EVERY `bus.fire('chat:end')` re-enters it. Fix = fire turn-error IN onChatEnd,
guarded by a new `wasInFlight` bool from `resolveWaiterFor` (true iff it resolved a
previously-UNSETTLED waiter) AND `outcome.kind !== 'complete'`. `wasInFlight`
cleanly avoids double/spurious fires: runner-reported live crash fires (waiter
unsettled); chokepoints skip (deferred settled before their bus.fire — their own
explicit fireTurnError stays the single fire); reaped-warm-runner late terminated
chat:end after a completed turn skips (no live waiter). IPC RESTAMPS ctx.reqId but
stamps ctx.conversationId, so the SSE turn-error subscriber now matches by **reqId
OR conversationId** (mirrors the done-frame turn-end subscriber). 3 early-spawn
returns (routed+fresh queue-work-failed, fresh sandbox-open-failed) got explicit
fireTurnError (reqId-matched; they unregister the waiter before chat:end so
onChatEnd skips them). onChatEnd → async; plugin.ts awaits it.

**F2a SPLIT (runner-side, deferred to TODO.md):** the retry's fresh runner crashes
exit 1 resuming the interrupted transcript. Explore confirmed the runner ALREADY
catches the resume exception and POSTs `event.chat-end{terminated}` before exit
(agent-claude-sdk-runner/main.ts:1191-1201) — so the user-visible stuck state is
the HOST not surfacing it, **which F2b fixes**. The crash root cause is inside the
SDK's `query({resume})` reading a truncated jsonl, NOT statically determinable;
needs cluster repro + image-rebuild loop ([[feedback_docker_build_cache_runner_fixes]])
+ a runner regression test. F2b is the safety net regardless. Faults B/D stay a
separate pre-existing finding ([[project_chat_qa_sweep_2026_05_23]]).

**Tests (RED-before):** agent-status (3), orchestrator (5: runner-reported fires +
carries conversationId, no-waiter + complete-with-waiter don't fire, 2 early-spawn
by reqId), sse (2: conversationId-match fires, different-conv+reqId doesn't).
**Gate:** build clean (tsc) + full repo test EXIT 0 (chat-orchestrator 71 /
channel-web 514, all 56 pkgs) + eslint clean on changed files.

**Decisions 60-64** (main checkout decisions.md). MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE F1+F2b walk +
F2a investigation are open TODO.md items.

content.text:
CodeRabbit found **one Major issue** (and it's correct): `resolveWaiterFor` collapsing to `boolean` throws away the original waiter reqId, forcing `onChatEnd` to fire with the IPC-restamped `ctx.reqId` → SSE falls back to the coarse conversationId match, which could close the **wrong** stream if two turns are in flight on one conversation.

The key insight in CodeRabbit's suggestion: `resolveWaiterFor` **already recovers the original reqId** (`firstReqId` in its sessionId fallback) — it just discards it. Returning it lets `onChatEnd` fire with the *precise original reqId*, so the SSE matches by reqId (no coarse conversationId match needed at all). This is strictly better. Let me verify the concurrent-invoke model the comment references, then adopt it.

content.content:
458	  // Waiters are tracked by ctx.reqId (server-minted, J9, unique per
459	  // agent:invoke). On the J6 routed path, two concurrent agent:invokes for the
460	  // same conversation share a sessionId — keying by sessionId would let
461	  // the second agent:invoke overwrite the first's waiter (causing the first
462	  // to time out and the second to resolve with the wrong outcome).
463	  //
464	  // Resolution paths:
465	  //   - chat:end fired by the orchestrator itself (error paths) carries
466	  //     the agent:invoke ctx → ctx.reqId matches directly.
467	  //   - chat:end fired by the IPC server (runner POSTs /event.chat-end)
468	  //     stamps a fresh per-request ctx.reqId, so the reqId lookup
469	  //     misses. We fall back via `reqIdsBySession`: when only one
470	  //     waiter exists for a given sessionId, resolve THAT waiter; when
471	  //     multiple exist (the routed-collision case), the reqId-keyed
472	  //     entry from the orchestrator self-fire wins. The fresh-spawn
473	  //     path always has exactly one waiter per sessionId.
474	  const waitersByReqId = new Map<string, Deferred<AgentOutcome>>();
475	  const reqIdsBySession = new Map<string, Set<string>>();
476	  function registerWaiter(
477	    sessionId: string,
478	    reqId: string,
479	    deferred: Deferred<AgentOutcome>,

content.text:
Confirmed — the comment (458-473) explicitly documents that two concurrent agent:invokes on one conversation **can share a sessionId** (the J6 routed-collision case). So CodeRabbit's concern is legitimate: conversationId-match is too coarse and could close a co-resident turn's stream. The precise fix — recover the original reqId (which `resolveWaiterFor` already computes) and fire with it, matching the SSE by reqId — is strictly better and lets me **revert the SSE conversationId-match entirely**. Adopting it.

Step 1 — `resolveWaiterFor` returns the resolved original reqId:

-  it('turn-error matched by conversationId (restamped reqId) emits an error frame and closes', async () => {
+  // F2b regression — turn-error matches by reqId ONLY, never by conversationId.
+  // A runner-reported terminated chat:end restamps ctx.reqId, but the
+  // orchestrator recovers the ORIGINAL agent:invoke reqId (resolveWaiterFor)
+  // and fires with that, so this stream still terminates on its own reqId. The
+  // important property: a turn-error for a DIFFERENT reqId on the SAME
+  // conversation must NOT close this stream (two concurrent invokes can share a
+  // conversation — a conversationId match would wrongly terminate the sibling).
+  it('turn-error for a DIFFERENT reqId on the SAME conversation does not close us', async () => {
const { bus, initCtx, handler, buffer } = bootHandler();
+      // A sibling turn on the same conversation errored — fire with its reqId
+      // and the same conversationId on ctx.
-        // reqId restamped by the IPC server → will NOT match 'r-test'.
-  it('turn-error on a DIFFERENT conversationId (and non-matching reqId) does not close us', async () => {
-    const { bus, initCtx, handler, buffer } = bootHandler();
+      // Our stream (r-test) stays open — only the sibling's stream should close.

content.text: Gate green: build clean (tsc), chat-orchestrator 71, channel-web 513 (net of removing one test), eslint clean. Commit the CodeRabbit fix as a targeted follow-up commit (per [[feedback_targeted_followup_commits]], not amend):

input.command:
git add packages/chat-orchestrator/src/orchestrator.ts packages/channel-web/src/server/sse.ts packages/chat-orchestrator/src/__tests__/orchestrator.test.ts packages/channel-web/src/__tests__/server/sse.test.ts && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(chat-orchestrator): fire F2b turn-error with the recovered original reqId (CodeRabbit)

CodeRabbit (Major): resolveWaiterFor collapsed to a boolean, discarding the
original waiter reqId — so onChatEnd fired chat:turn-error with the IPC-restamped
ctx.reqId and the SSE had to fall back to a conversationId match. That's too
coarse: two concurrent agent:invokes can share a conversation (and even a
sessionId — see the waiter-map comment), so a conversationId match could
terminate the WRONG stream.

resolveWaiterFor already computes the original reqId in its sessionId fallback
(firstReqId) — it just threw it away. Return it (string | undefined) and fire the
turn-error with that precise per-turn key. The SSE reverts to reqId-only matching
(its #137 behavior), so a turn-error never closes a co-resident turn's stream.

Tests: orchestrator runner-reported case now asserts the turn-error fires with the
recovered ORIGINAL reqId (not the restamped one); sse gains a regression test that
a turn-error for a different reqId on the SAME conversation does NOT close the
stream (CodeRabbit's requested same-conversation/different-reqId case).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
echo "=== committed ===" && git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
